arms-barbell-curl.name: Barbell Curl
instructions: Stand holding a barbell with an underhand grip about shoulder-width and the bar against the thighs.
Keep the upper arms pinned to the sides and brace the trunk tall.
Curl the bar to the fronts of the shoulders by bending only at the elbows, then lower to full extension.
Keep the wrists aligned with the forearms and avoid swinging the torso.
Do not let the elbows drift forward to shorten the eccentric.

arms-bayesian-curl.name: Bayesian Curl
instructions: Attach a single handle to the low pulley and stand facing away with the working arm reaching slightly behind the torso.
Take a staggered stance for balance and brace the trunk.
Curl the handle forward by bending the elbow while keeping the upper arm still, then return to the stretched start.
Keep continuous cable tension and a neutral wrist.
Finish the reps on one arm, then switch the handle and stance to the other side.

arms-bench-dip.name: Bench Dip
instructions: Sit on the edge of a stable bench, place both hands beside the hips on the bench, and walk the feet forward.
Support yourself on straight arms with the hips just in front of the bench edge.
Bend the elbows to lower the body until the upper arms are near parallel, then press back up.
Keep the shoulders down and the elbows tracking back rather than flaring wide.
Bend the knees more to make it easier, or elevate the feet only if the shoulders stay comfortable.

arms-cable-curl.name: Cable Curl
instructions: Attach a straight or EZ bar to the low pulley and stand facing the stack with the cable taut.
Take an underhand grip, pin the upper arms to the sides, and stand tall.
Curl the bar toward the shoulders, squeeze, then lower to full arm extension against the cable.
Keep the elbows still and the wrists neutral.
Avoid leaning back to finish the curl as the stack gets heavy.

safety.title: Pulley Height
safety.description: Set the pulley low so the cable travels upward through the curl without yanking your shoulders forward.

safety.title: Handle Attachment
safety.description: Use a straight or EZ bar attachment clipped firmly; confirm the pin is fully seated in the stack.

safety.title: Stance & Distance
safety.description: Stand far enough that the weight stays clear of the stack at the bottom, elbows close to your sides.

safety.title: Body Alignment
safety.description: Keep a tall torso and soft knees; avoid leaning back to swing the load.

arms-cable-overhead-tricep-extension.name: Cable Overhead Tricep Extension
instructions: Attach a rope to the high pulley, face away from the stack, and hold both rope ends behind the head with elbows bent.
Take a staggered stance, brace the trunk, and keep the upper arms beside the ears.
Extend the elbows until the arms are straight overhead, then return the rope behind the head.
Keep the elbows pointed forward/up and minimize upper-arm travel.
Use a load you can control without arching the lower back.

safety.title: Pulley Height
safety.description: Set the pulley high so the rope or bar starts above your head with tension already on the cable.

safety.title: Handle Attachment
safety.description: Clip a rope or angled bar securely; keep hands clear of the stack path.

safety.title: Starting Position
safety.description: Step forward until elbows point forward and upper arms stay beside your ears without shrugging.

safety.title: Body Alignment
safety.description: Brace your core and keep ribs down so the low back does not arch under the stretch.

arms-close-grip-bench-press.name: Close-Grip Bench Press
instructions: Lie on a flat bench under a barbell and take an overhand grip about shoulder-width or slightly narrower.
Plant both feet, set the shoulder blades, and unrack the bar over the lower chest.
Lower the bar to the lower chest with elbows tracking close to the torso, then press to straight arms.
Keep the wrists stacked over the elbows and the hips on the bench.
Do not take an extremely narrow grip that stresses the wrists.

safety.title: J-Hook Height
safety.description: Set J-hooks so you unrack with nearly locked elbows without reaching high.

safety.title: Safety Arms
safety.description: Place safeties just below the lowest controlled position over your lower chest.

safety.title: Grip Width
safety.description: Keep hands about shoulder-width; wrists stay stacked over elbows, not flared wide.

safety.title: Foot Placement
safety.description: Plant both feet flat to brace without bouncing the hips off the bench.

safety.title: Shoulder Position
safety.description: Set the shoulder blades back and down on the bench before you unrack.

arms-close-grip-push-up.name: Close-Grip Push-Up
instructions: Place both hands on the floor closer than shoulder-width, roughly under the chest.
Extend into a plank with the body in one straight line and the core braced.
Lower the chest by bending the elbows close to the ribs, then press back up.
Keep the elbows tracking back rather than flaring to 90°.
Drop to the knees if needed while keeping the same close hand placement.

arms-concentration-curl.name: Concentration Curl
instructions: Sit on a bench, hold a dumbbell in one hand, and brace that upper arm against the inner thigh of the same-side leg.
Plant both feet wide for a stable base and lean slightly forward.
Curl the dumbbell toward the shoulder without moving the upper arm off the thigh, then lower fully.
Keep the wrist neutral and the non-working hand on the opposite knee for balance.
Finish the reps on one arm, then switch the dumbbell and brace the other arm on the other thigh.

arms-cross-body-hammer-curl.name: Cross-Body Hammer Curl
instructions: Stand holding dumbbells at the sides with a neutral hammer grip.
Keeping the palm facing the body, curl one dumbbell across toward the opposite shoulder.
Squeeze at the top without swinging the torso, then lower with control.
Alternate arms or finish one side before switching.
Keep the working elbow close to the ribs and avoid shrugging.

arms-dip.name: Dip
instructions: Grip parallel bars and support yourself on straight arms with the torso more upright than a chest dip.
Keep the legs still and the shoulders set down away from the ears.
Bend the elbows to lower until the upper arms are near parallel, then press to straight arms.
Keep the elbows closer to the body to emphasize the triceps.
Stop if the shoulders feel unstable or pinched at the bottom.

arms-drag-curl.name: Drag Curl
instructions: Stand holding a barbell or dumbbells with an underhand grip at the thighs.
Brace the trunk and keep the elbows close as you begin the curl.
Drag the weight up along the torso by pulling the elbows back, not swinging them forward.
Squeeze at about lower-chest height, then lower along the same path.
Use less load than a standard curl; keep the bar close to the body the whole time.

arms-dumbbell-curl.name: Dumbbell Curl
instructions: Stand holding a dumbbell in each hand at the sides with palms facing forward or neutral.
Pin the upper arms to the sides and stand tall with the trunk braced.
Curl both dumbbells to the shoulders, squeeze, then lower to full extension.
You may rotate the palms up during the curl if starting from a hammer position.
Do not swing the hips or shrug the shoulders to move the weight.

arms-ez-bar-curl.name: EZ-Bar Curl
instructions: Stand holding an EZ bar on the angled underhand grips with the bar against the thighs.
Keep the upper arms still at the sides and brace the trunk.
Curl the bar to the fronts of the shoulders, then lower to full extension.
Let the EZ angles keep the wrists comfortable without forcing them straight.
Avoid torso swing and keep the elbows from traveling far forward.

arms-hammer-curl.name: Hammer Curl
instructions: Stand holding a dumbbell in each hand at the sides with palms facing each other.
Keep the upper arms pinned to the torso and stand tall.
Curl the dumbbells toward the shoulders without rotating the palms, then lower fully.
Keep the wrists neutral and the elbows fixed at the sides.
Do not swing the weights or lean back to finish the curl.

arms-incline-dumbbell-curl.name: Incline Dumbbell Curl
instructions: Set a bench to a moderate incline, sit back, and let a dumbbell hang in each hand with palms forward.
Keep the upper arms hanging straight down beside the torso and the head against the pad.
Curl both dumbbells without letting the upper arms swing forward, then lower to a full stretch.
Maintain contact of the upper arms with the sides of the body through most of the lift.
Use a lighter load than standing curls because the stretch position is more demanding.

arms-jm-press.name: JM Press
instructions: Lie on a flat bench holding a barbell or EZ bar over the upper chest with a close overhand grip.
Plant both feet and set the shoulder blades on the bench.
Lower the bar toward the upper chest/neck while the elbows travel forward and down, then press back up.
Keep the movement between a close-grip press and a skull crusher rather than flaring wide.
Start light—this hybrid stresses the elbows when rushed.

arms-kickback.name: Tricep Kickback
instructions: Hold a dumbbell in one hand, hinge at the hips, and support the torso with the free hand on a bench or knee.
Pin the working upper arm to the side with the elbow bent about 90°.
Extend the elbow until the arm is straight behind you, squeeze the tricep, then return to 90°.
Keep the upper arm still and the torso quiet.
Finish the reps on one arm, then switch the dumbbell and supporting hand to the other side.

arms-machine-bicep-curl.name: Machine Bicep Curl
instructions: Adjust the preacher or curl-machine pad so the upper arms rest fully on it with the elbows aligned to the pivot.
Sit or stand as the machine requires and take an underhand grip on the handles.
Curl the handles toward the shoulders, squeeze, then lower to full extension without locking harshly.
Keep the upper arms glued to the pad throughout.
Avoid lifting the elbows or hips to yank the stack.

safety.title: Seat Height
safety.description: Set the seat so your elbows line up with the machine’s pivot and the pad supports your upper arms.

safety.title: Arm Pad Position
safety.description: Rest the backs of your upper arms fully on the pad; shoulders should stay down, not shrugged.

safety.title: Handle Starting Position
safety.description: Handles should begin with elbows nearly extended but not forced into a painful lockout.

safety.title: Adjustment Pins
safety.description: Fully engage every seat and pad pin before selecting the weight stack.

arms-machine-tricep-extension.name: Machine Tricep Extension
instructions: Adjust the seat so the machine pads or handles align with your elbows when the upper arms are supported.
Grip the handles and brace lightly against the pad.
Extend the elbows to straighten the arms against the machine resistance.
Pause briefly without locking aggressively, then return to a controlled stretch.
Keep the upper arms still; only the forearms should move.

safety.title: Seat Height
safety.description: Adjust the seat so your elbows align with the machine pivot through the whole press.

safety.title: Back Support
safety.description: Sit tall with your back against the pad so the torso does not rock to finish reps.

safety.title: Handle Alignment
safety.description: Start with handles near your forehead or upper chest along the machine’s path—keep elbows fixed.

safety.title: Foot Placement
safety.description: Plant both feet flat for a stable base before pressing the stack.

arms-overhead-tricep-extension.name: Overhead Tricep Extension
instructions: Sit or stand holding one dumbbell overhead with both hands under the top plate, arms straight.
Brace the trunk and keep the upper arms beside the ears.
Bend the elbows to lower the dumbbell behind the head, then extend back to the top.
Keep the elbows pointing forward/up and minimize upper-arm flare.
Use a load you can control without arching the lower back or hitting the neck.

arms-preacher-curl.name: Preacher Curl
instructions: Sit at a preacher bench and rest both upper arms fully on the angled pad with an EZ or straight bar in an underhand grip.
Start with the arms extended but not forcefully locked.
Curl the bar toward the shoulders, squeeze, then lower slowly to full extension.
Keep the armpits and upper arms in contact with the pad the entire time.
Do not lift the elbows off the pad or rock the torso to finish the rep.

safety.title: Seat Height
safety.description: Raise or lower the seat so your armpits sit at the top edge of the pad without shrugging.

safety.title: Pad Position
safety.description: Upper arms should lie fully on the pad from armpit to elbow; wrists stay neutral on the bar.

safety.title: Starting Range
safety.description: Begin with arms nearly straight but stop short of hyperextending the elbows at the bottom.

safety.title: Bar Path
safety.description: Keep elbows planted on the pad; do not lift them to cheat the weight up.

arms-reverse-curl.name: Reverse Curl
instructions: Stand holding a barbell or EZ bar with an overhand grip about shoulder-width against the thighs.
Pin the upper arms to the sides and stand tall.
Curl the bar toward the shoulders without flipping the palms, then lower to full extension.
Keep the wrists firm and aligned with the forearms.
Use a lighter load than underhand curls—forearms often limit this variation first.

arms-reverse-wrist-curl.name: Reverse Wrist Curl
instructions: Sit on a bench holding a light barbell or dumbbells with an overhand grip and the forearms resting on the thighs or a pad, wrists hanging off.
Keep the forearms glued down and the grip secure.
Extend the wrists to raise the back of the hands, pause, then lower into a stretch.
Move only at the wrists—do not lift the forearms off the support.
Use a slow tempo; bouncing easily irritates the wrists.

arms-rope-pushdown.name: Rope Pushdown
instructions: Attach a rope to the high pulley and stand facing the stack with elbows pinned to the sides.
Hold both rope ends with palms facing each other and a slight forward lean if needed.
Extend the elbows to push the rope down, spread the ends apart at the bottom, then return to about 90°.
Keep the upper arms still and the wrists neutral.
Do not let the elbows flare wide or the torso rock to move the stack.

safety.title: Pulley Height
safety.description: Set the pulley high so the rope hangs at about upper-chest height before you take tension.

safety.title: Handle Attachment
safety.description: Attach a rope and confirm the clip is closed before selecting weight.

safety.title: Starting Position
safety.description: Stand close enough that elbows stay pinned to your sides from the first inch of motion.

safety.title: Body Alignment
safety.description: Keep a slight forward lean from the hips only; do not turn the pushdown into a crunch.

arms-single-arm-cable-pushdown.name: Single-Arm Cable Pushdown
instructions: Attach a single handle to a high cable and stand facing the stack with a soft forward lean.
Pin the working elbow to the side with the forearm starting near the upper arm.
Extend the elbow to push the handle down until the arm is straight.
Squeeze the triceps, then return slowly to the start without letting the elbow drift forward.
Finish the set on one arm before switching; keep the shoulder quiet.

safety.title: Pulley Height
safety.description: Set the pulley high so the single handle starts near shoulder height with light preload.

safety.title: Handle Attachment
safety.description: Use a D-handle clipped securely; check the carabiner before each set.

safety.title: Starting Position
safety.description: Square your shoulders to the stack and keep the working elbow fixed beside your ribcage.

safety.title: Distance from Machine
safety.description: Stand just far enough that the cable clears your body without pulling you sideways.

arms-skull-crusher.name: Skull Crusher
instructions: Lie on a flat bench holding an EZ bar or dumbbells over the shoulders with arms straight.
Plant both feet and keep the upper arms roughly perpendicular to the floor.
Bend the elbows to lower the weight toward the forehead or just behind the head, then extend back to the top.
Keep the upper arms fixed—only the forearms should move.
Start light and control the eccentric to protect the elbows.

arms-spider-curl.name: Spider Curl
instructions: Lie chest-down on an incline bench so both arms hang straight toward the floor holding a barbell or dumbbells.
Let the upper arms hang perpendicular to the floor and take an underhand grip.
Curl the weight toward the shoulders without swinging the upper arms forward, then lower fully.
Keep the chest glued to the pad throughout the set.
Use a lighter load than standing curls because peak contraction is stricter here.

arms-tate-press.name: Tate Press
instructions: Lie on a flat bench holding dumbbells above the chest with palms facing each other or slightly toward the feet.
Keep the upper arms angled so the elbows point somewhat out.
Bend the elbows to lower the inner plates toward the center of the chest.
Extend the elbows to press the dumbbells back up without flaring the elbows excessively.
Use a moderate load and a controlled tempo; stop if the elbows or shoulders feel stressed.

arms-tricep-pushdown.name: Tricep Pushdown
instructions: Attach a straight or angled bar to the high pulley and stand facing the stack with elbows at the sides.
Grip the bar with palms down and brace the trunk tall.
Extend the elbows to push the bar down until the arms are straight, then return to about 90°.
Keep the upper arms glued to the torso and the wrists straight.
Avoid leaning hard into the bar or flaring the elbows wide.

safety.title: Pulley Height
safety.description: Set the pulley high so the bar rests near upper chest before you drive down.

safety.title: Handle Attachment
safety.description: Clip a straight or V-bar firmly and fully insert the weight-stack pin.

safety.title: Starting Position
safety.description: Take a shoulder-width stance and pin both elbows to your sides before pressing down.

safety.title: Body Alignment
safety.description: Keep the torso quiet; use only the elbows to move the bar, not a hip thrust.

arms-weighted-dip.name: Weighted Dip
instructions: Secure a dip belt with plates or hold a dumbbell between the feet, then grip parallel bars.
Support yourself on straight arms with a more upright torso for triceps emphasis.
Lower until the upper arms are near parallel, then press to straight arms.
Keep the added load still so it does not swing forward.
Start lighter than bodyweight dips and stop if the shoulders feel unstable.

arms-wrist-curl.name: Wrist Curl
instructions: Sit on a bench holding a light barbell or dumbbells with an underhand grip and the forearms on the thighs or a pad, wrists hanging off.
Keep the forearms glued to the support and the grip secure.
Flex the wrists to curl the palms up, pause, then lower into a stretch.
Move only at the wrists without lifting the forearms.
Use a slow tempo and stop if the wrists feel sharp pain rather than muscle work.

arms-zottman-curl.name: Zottman Curl
instructions: Stand holding dumbbells at the sides with palms facing forward.
Curl the weights up with a standard supinated grip, elbows pinned to the sides.
At the top, rotate the wrists so palms face down.
Lower slowly with the pronated grip, then rotate palms forward at the bottom for the next rep.
Keep the torso still and use a weight you can control on the eccentric.

back-assisted-pull-up.name: Assisted Pull-Up
instructions: Set the assisted pull-up machine knee pad and select a counterweight that lets you finish controlled reps.
Kneel on the pad, take an overhand shoulder-width grip, and hang with arms straight.
Pull the chest toward the bar by driving the elbows down, then lower to a full hang.
Keep the shoulders active and avoid swinging or kipping with the knees.
Reduce assistance as you get stronger rather than rushing unfinished reps.

safety.title: Knee / Foot Pad
safety.description: Set the assist pad so knees or feet rest securely without sliding off during the pull.

safety.title: Assist Load
safety.description: Choose enough assistance to complete controlled reps—avoid bouncing off the pad at the bottom.

safety.title: Grip & Alignment
safety.description: Take an even grip and keep the body square to the bar; do not twist on the assist pad.

safety.title: Shoulder Start
safety.description: Begin from a dead hang with shoulders packed down before the first pull.

back-barbell-row.name: Barbell Row
instructions: Stand over a loaded barbell with feet about hip-width and the bar over mid-foot.
Hinge at the hips to a torso angle near 45°, brace the trunk, and take an even overhand grip.
Pull the bar to the lower ribs by driving the elbows back, pause, then lower to full hang.
Keep the spine long and the knees softly bent throughout.
Do not turn the row into a shrug or use a hip snap to yank the bar up.

back-barbell-shrug.name: Barbell Shrug
instructions: Stand tall holding a loaded barbell in front of the thighs with an overhand grip.
Keep the arms straight, feet about hip-width, and the trunk braced.
Elevate both shoulders straight toward the ears, pause briefly, then lower slowly.
Keep the neck long and avoid jutting the chin forward.
Do not roll the shoulders in circles or bounce the bar off the thighs.

back-chest-supported-row.name: Chest-Supported Row
instructions: Set a chest-supported row bench or incline bench so your chest rests fully on the pad.
Hold a barbell or dumbbells with arms hanging straight and feet planted for balance.
Pull the elbows back toward the hips until the shoulder blades retract, then lower with control.
Keep the chest glued to the pad so the lower back cannot cheat the lift.
Choose a pad angle that lets the arms hang freely without scraping the floor.

safety.title: Pad Angle / Height
safety.description: Set the chest pad so your torso is supported with the sternum on the pad and neck neutral.

safety.title: Seat or Stance
safety.description: Adjust seat or foot platform so arms hang long without the shoulders jamming into the pad.

safety.title: Starting Position
safety.description: Let the weights hang with soft elbows, then row by driving elbows back toward the hips.

safety.title: Head Position
safety.description: Keep the neck long—do not crank the head up to watch yourself in the mirror.

back-chin-up.name: Chin-Up
instructions: Hang from a pull-up bar with an underhand grip about shoulder-width apart and arms straight.
Brace the trunk and start with the shoulders gently pulled down away from the ears.
Pull until the chin clears the bar by driving the elbows down and back, then lower to a full hang.
Keep the legs quiet and avoid swinging or kipping.
Use a band or assisted machine if you cannot complete a controlled full-range chin-up.

back-close-grip-lat-pulldown.name: Close-Grip Lat Pulldown
instructions: Attach a close parallel or V-handle to the high pulley and secure both thighs under the pad.
Sit tall, take the handles with a neutral grip, and start with arms fully extended.
Pull the handle to the upper chest by driving the elbows down beside the torso, then return slowly.
Keep the chest lifted and avoid leaning far back to finish the rep.
Do not pull the handle behind the neck.

safety.title: Thigh Pad
safety.description: Set the thigh pad snug so your hips stay down through the full pull.

safety.title: Seat Height
safety.description: Sit so the close-grip handle reaches full overhead extension without shrugging.

safety.title: Grip
safety.description: Use a neutral or close grip with wrists stacked; elbows drive down toward the ribs.

safety.title: Torso Angle
safety.description: Keep the chest up and a small lean; pull the handle to the upper chest, not behind the neck.

back-deadlift.name: Deadlift
instructions: Stand with mid-foot under a loaded barbell, feet about hip-width, and the bar close to the shins.
Hinge and bend the knees to grip the bar just outside the legs, then brace and set a neutral spine.
Push the floor away to stand tall, keeping the bar close, then reverse the motion to the floor.
Keep the hips and shoulders rising together rather than the hips shooting up first.
Reset each rep from a dead stop if form breaks, and never round hard through the lower back.

back-dumbbell-row.name: Dumbbell Row
instructions: Place one hand and the same-side knee on a flat bench, with the other foot on the floor and a dumbbell in the free hand.
Keep the back roughly parallel to the floor and the working arm hanging straight under the shoulder.
Row the dumbbell to the hip by driving the elbow back, pause, then lower fully.
Keep the hips and shoulders square to the bench—do not twist open.
Finish the set, then switch the supporting side and row with the other arm.

back-dumbbell-shrug.name: Dumbbell Shrug
instructions: Stand tall holding a dumbbell in each hand at the sides with arms straight.
Brace the trunk and keep the feet about hip-width apart.
Shrug both shoulders straight up toward the ears, pause, then lower slowly.
Keep the arms hanging and the neck long without jutting the chin.
Avoid rolling the shoulders or using a knee bounce to start the shrug.

back-face-pull.name: Face Pull
instructions: Attach a rope to a cable pulley set around upper-chest or face height.
Stand facing the stack with a soft knee bend and hold both rope ends with palms facing down or in.
Pull the rope toward the face while flaring the elbows out and externally rotating the shoulders.
Finish with the hands beside the ears and the rear shoulders squeezed, then return slowly.
Use a light enough load to keep the neck relaxed and the elbows high.

safety.title: Pulley Height
safety.description: Set the pulley at about upper-chest to face height so the rope travels back toward the face.

safety.title: Handle Attachment
safety.description: Clip a rope securely; take an even grip on both ends before stepping back.

safety.title: Distance from Machine
safety.description: Step back until there is light tension at arm’s length without leaning excessively.

safety.title: Body Alignment
safety.description: Keep ribs down and pull the rope toward the face with elbows high—avoid shrugging the traps hard.

back-good-morning.name: Good Morning
instructions: Set a barbell across the upper back as in a back squat and stand with feet about hip-width.
Brace the trunk hard and unlock the knees slightly while keeping them fixed.
Hinge at the hips to lower the torso forward, then drive the hips forward to stand tall.
Keep the bar locked on the back and the spine neutral throughout the hinge.
Use a shallow range first—this lift loads the lower back heavily when form slips.

back-hyperextension.name: Back Extension
instructions: Adjust the back-extension bench so the hip pad sits just below the hip crease and the ankles are secured.
Cross the arms on the chest or hold a plate at the chest and start with the torso roughly in line with the legs.
Hinge at the hips to lower the torso under control, then extend the hips to return without over-arching.
Keep the neck neutral as an extension of the spine.
Stop short of hyperextending the lower back at the top of each rep.

safety.title: Pad Height
safety.description: Set the thigh pad just below the hips so you can hinge freely without pressure on the kneecaps.

safety.title: Ankle / Foot Support
safety.description: Secure the ankles or feet so the body cannot slide forward when you hinge down.

safety.title: Hip Position
safety.description: Place the hips at the pad edge so the hinge happens at the hips, not a rounded low back.

safety.title: Range of Motion
safety.description: Lower only as far as you can keep a neutral spine; stop short of pain or excessive rounding.

back-inverted-row.name: Inverted Row
instructions: Set a secure bar around waist height in a rack or use a sturdy smith bar.
Lie under the bar, take an overhand grip, and hang with the body straight and heels on the floor.
Pull the chest to the bar by driving the elbows back, then lower to full arm extension.
Keep the hips up so the body stays in one line from shoulders to heels.
Raise the bar or bend the knees to make it easier; lower the bar to make it harder.

back-kroc-row.name: Kroc Row
instructions: Brace one hand and knee on a bench, holding a heavy dumbbell in the free hand with the torso nearly parallel to the floor.
Start with a long arm and a packed working shoulder.
Row the dumbbell hard toward the hip with a powerful but controlled pull, allowing a small torso rock if needed for heavy loads.
Lower under control to a full stretch, then repeat for higher-rep sets.
Keep the neck neutral and do not twist so far that the low back rounds.

back-lat-pulldown.name: Lat Pulldown
instructions: Attach a wide lat bar to the high pulley and secure both thighs under the pad.
Sit tall, take a wide overhand grip, and start with arms fully extended overhead.
Pull the bar to the upper chest by driving the elbows down and slightly back, then return slowly.
Lead with the elbows and keep the chest lifted rather than curling the spine hard.
Never pull the bar behind the neck.

safety.title: Thigh Pad
safety.description: Adjust the thigh pad so your legs stay secured when you pull—pad snug on the quads without pain.

safety.title: Seat Height
safety.description: Sit tall so you can fully extend the arms overhead without the pad lifting you off the seat.

safety.title: Grip
safety.description: Take a grip that lets elbows travel down and back; avoid an extreme wide hang that stresses the shoulders.

safety.title: Torso Angle
safety.description: Keep a slight lean back from the hips; do not yank the bar behind the neck.

safety.title: Starting Shoulder Position
safety.description: Begin with arms long and scapulae gently set—depress the shoulders before the first pull.

back-machine-pullover.name: Machine Pullover
instructions: Sit in a pullover machine with the pads against the upper arms or elbows per the machine design.
Brace the trunk against the pad and keep the ribs down.
Start with the arms overhead and pull the lever in an arc down toward the hips.
Pause briefly with the elbows near the sides, then return slowly to a comfortable stretch.
Do not hyperextend the lower back or let the weight stack slam at the top.

safety.title: Seat Height
safety.description: Sit so the elbow pads or handles align with your shoulders at the start of the arc.

safety.title: Back Support
safety.description: Keep the back against the pad; do not arch aggressively to finish the pullover.

safety.title: Starting Range
safety.description: Start with arms overhead only as far as shoulders stay comfortable—stop before pain.

safety.title: Adjustment Pins
safety.description: Fully engage seat and ROM pins before selecting the stack.

back-machine-row.name: Machine Row
instructions: Adjust the chest pad and seat so the handles align with the mid-torso when your chest is supported.
Place both feet on the platform or floor and grip the chosen handles evenly.
Pull the handles toward the body until the shoulder blades retract, then return with control.
Keep the chest on the pad and avoid using a torso rock to finish the pull.
Select a handle position that lets the elbows travel beside the ribs without shrugging.

safety.title: Seat Height
safety.description: Set the seat so handles meet mid-torso and your chest sits squarely on the pad.

safety.title: Chest Pad
safety.description: Adjust the chest pad for firm contact without forcing a rounded upper back.

safety.title: Foot Support
safety.description: Plant feet on the platform or floor so hips stay stable through the row.

safety.title: Handle Starting Position
safety.description: Begin with arms extended and shoulders reaching; row by driving elbows back, not shrugging up.

back-meadows-row.name: Meadows Row
instructions: Secure a barbell in a landmine base and stand perpendicular to the bar beside the loaded end.
Hinge at the hips, brace the trunk, and grip the sleeve of the bar with the hand farther from the base.
Row the bar toward the hip by driving that elbow back, then lower until the arm is straight.
Keep the hips square and the non-working hand braced on the thigh or a bench if needed.
Finish the reps on one side, then switch sides and face the opposite direction.

back-neutral-grip-pull-up.name: Neutral-Grip Pull-Up
instructions: Hang from parallel neutral-grip handles with arms straight and palms facing each other.
Brace the trunk and set the shoulders slightly down away from the ears.
Pull until the chin clears the handles, then lower to a full hang under control.
Keep the elbows close to the body and avoid swinging the legs.
Stop short of unfinished reps if the shoulders lose their active set position.

back-pendlay-row.name: Pendlay Row
instructions: Stand over a loaded barbell with mid-foot under the bar and a hinged torso roughly parallel to the floor.
Grip the bar just outside the legs, brace hard, and start each rep from a complete dead stop on the floor.
Explosively row the bar to the lower chest or upper abdomen, then lower it all the way back to the floor.
Keep the back flat and the hips fixed during the pull—only the arms and upper back move the bar.
Reset your brace between reps rather than bouncing the bar off the floor.

back-pull-up.name: Pull-Up
instructions: Hang from a pull-up bar with an overhand grip slightly wider than the shoulders and arms straight.
Brace the trunk and pull the shoulders gently down before bending the elbows.
Pull until the chin clears the bar, then lower to a full hang without swinging.
Drive the elbows down and keep the chest reaching toward the bar.
Use assistance if needed; avoid half-reps and kipping unless that is the intended variation.

back-rack-pull.name: Rack Pull
instructions: Set safety pins in a rack so a loaded barbell rests around knee height, then stand with mid-foot under the bar.
Grip the bar just outside the legs, brace, and set a neutral spine with the shoulders over the bar.
Drive through the floor to stand tall, lock the hips, then lower the bar back to the pins with control.
Keep the bar close to the body for the entire short pull.
Do not bounce the bar off the pins; each rep should start from a settled stop.

back-reverse-grip-lat-pulldown.name: Reverse-Grip Lat Pulldown
instructions: Attach a straight bar to the high pulley, take an underhand shoulder-width grip, and secure the thighs under the pad.
Sit tall with arms fully extended and the chest lifted.
Pull the bar to the upper chest by driving the elbows down beside the torso, then return slowly.
Keep the wrists aligned with the forearms and avoid excessive torso lean.
Do not finish by curling the bar with the biceps alone or pulling behind the neck.

safety.title: Thigh Pad
safety.description: Secure the thigh pad so underhand pulls cannot lift you off the seat.

safety.title: Seat Height
safety.description: Sit tall with arms fully extended at the top without forced shoulder elevation.

safety.title: Grip
safety.description: Take an underhand grip about shoulder-width; keep elbows close as you pull to the upper chest.

safety.title: Starting Shoulder Position
safety.description: Depress the shoulders before pulling; avoid finishing by shrugging the bar down.

back-seal-row.name: Seal Row
instructions: Lie face-down on a raised flat or seal-row bench with a barbell or dumbbells hanging freely below.
Let the chest and hips stay fully supported and take an even grip on the weight.
Row the weight toward the underside of the bench by driving the elbows up, then lower to a full hang.
Keep the head neutral and avoid lifting the chest off the pad.
Use a bench high enough that the plates clear the floor at the bottom.

safety.title: Bench Height
safety.description: Set the bench high enough that arms hang free without plates hitting the floor at the bottom.

safety.title: Body Position
safety.description: Lie prone with the chest centered; head stays neutral off the end of the bench if needed.

safety.title: Starting Position
safety.description: Begin with arms long under the shoulders; row without yanking the torso off the pad.

back-seated-cable-row.name: Seated Cable Row
instructions: Attach a V-handle or straight bar to the low pulley and sit with both feet on the platform and knees softly bent.
Sit tall, arms extended, and brace before the pull.
Pull the handle to the lower ribs while keeping the torso nearly upright, then return to full arm extension.
Retract the shoulder blades at the end of the pull without shrugging upward.
Do not use a large rock of the torso to yank the stack.

safety.title: Seat & Chest Pad
safety.description: If a chest pad is present, set it so your torso is tall with light contact—not crushed forward.

safety.title: Foot Support
safety.description: Place feet on the platform with soft knees; do not lock the knees hard against the plate.

safety.title: Handle Starting Position
safety.description: Start with arms long and shoulders reaching forward slightly, then set the scapulae before rowing.

safety.title: Body Alignment
safety.description: Keep a neutral spine; avoid extreme layback to yank the handle into the ribs.

back-single-arm-cable-row.name: Single-Arm Cable Row
instructions: Attach a single handle to the low pulley and stand or half-kneel facing the stack with the cable taut.
Hold the handle in one hand, brace the trunk, and start with the arm reaching forward.
Row the elbow back toward the hip, pause, then return to full reach.
Keep the hips and shoulders square—do not rotate open with the working side.
Complete the reps on one arm, then switch hands and match the stance on the other side.

safety.title: Pulley Height
safety.description: Set the pulley at about mid-torso height for a horizontal row path.

safety.title: Handle Attachment
safety.description: Clip a D-handle securely and confirm the stack pin is fully inserted.

safety.title: Stance
safety.description: Use a staggered stance for balance; square the shoulders as you row the elbow back.

safety.title: Body Alignment
safety.description: Avoid rotating the torso to finish; keep the working shoulder packed down.

back-single-arm-lat-pulldown.name: Single-Arm Lat Pulldown
instructions: Attach a single handle to a high cable and sit with thighs locked under the pad.
Reach up with one hand, arm long, and brace the trunk square to the stack.
Pull the handle down beside the ribcage by driving the elbow toward the hip.
Pause with the shoulder blade down and back, then return to a full stretch overhead.
Finish all reps on one side before switching; avoid twisting the torso for momentum.

safety.title: Thigh Pad
safety.description: Keep the thigh pad snug so a one-arm pull does not twist you off the seat.

safety.title: Pulley / Handle
safety.description: Clip a single handle high; confirm the carabiner is closed before loading the stack.

safety.title: Torso Alignment
safety.description: Square the hips to the stack; resist rotating the torso to finish the rep.

safety.title: Starting Shoulder Position
safety.description: Begin with the working arm long and the shoulder packed down before pulling.

back-straight-arm-pulldown.name: Straight-Arm Pulldown
instructions: Attach a straight bar or rope to the high pulley and stand facing the stack with a slight hip hinge.
Hold the attachment with arms nearly straight and the cable already under tension.
Sweep the attachment down in an arc toward the thighs by moving at the shoulders, then return slowly.
Keep a soft fixed elbow bend and brace the ribs down so the lower back does not arch.
Stop if you must bend the elbows hard or swing the torso to move the weight.

safety.title: Pulley Height
safety.description: Set the pulley high so the bar or rope starts overhead with arms long.

safety.title: Handle Attachment
safety.description: Clip a straight bar or rope firmly; keep hands clear of the stack.

safety.title: Starting Position
safety.description: Hinge slightly at the hips with soft elbows locked in that angle through the arc.

safety.title: Body Alignment
safety.description: Keep ribs down; move from the shoulders and lats—do not turn it into a crunch.

back-sumo-deadlift.name: Sumo Deadlift
instructions: Stand with a wide stance over a loaded barbell, toes turned out, and the bar over mid-foot.
Sit the hips down between the knees, grip the bar inside the legs, and brace with a neutral spine.
Push the floor away to stand tall, keeping the bar close, then lower with control.
Keep the knees tracking over the toes and the chest facing forward.
Do not let the hips shoot up first or the lower back round under the load.

back-t-bar-row.name: T-Bar Row
instructions: Load a T-bar or landmine bar and straddle it, hinging at the hips to grip the handles.
Keep the torso braced near a 45° hinge with knees softly bent.
Pull the handles to the lower chest or upper abdomen, pause, then lower to full hang.
Keep the elbows close and the spine long without rounding the lower back.
Avoid yanking with a hip snap; the torso angle should stay nearly fixed.

back-trap-bar-deadlift.name: Trap Bar Deadlift
instructions: Stand inside a loaded trap bar with feet about hip-width and the handles aligned beside the mid-foot.
Sit the hips down, grip both handles evenly, and brace with a neutral spine.
Push the floor away to stand tall, then lower the bar until the plates touch down under control.
Keep the arms straight and the chest up as the hips and shoulders rise together.
Use the high or low handles consistently within a set and never round hard through the back.

back-weighted-chin-up.name: Weighted Chin-Up
instructions: Secure a dip belt with plates or hold a dumbbell between the feet, then take an underhand pull-up grip.
Hang with arms straight and the added load hanging still between the legs.
Pull until the chin clears the bar, then lower to a full hang under control.
Keep the shoulders set and avoid swinging the plates or dumbbell.
Choose a load that still allows a full controlled range—partial weighted reps teach poor timing.

back-weighted-pull-up.name: Weighted Pull-Up
instructions: Secure a dip belt with plates or hold a dumbbell between the feet, then take an overhand pull-up grip.
Hang with arms straight and start with the shoulders gently pulled down.
Pull until the chin clears the bar, then lower to a full hang without swinging.
Keep the load centered so it does not pull you forward or sideways.
Reduce the added load if you cannot control the bottom half of the descent.

back-wide-grip-pull-up.name: Wide-Grip Pull-Up
instructions: Hang from a pull-up bar with an overhand grip clearly wider than shoulder width.
Start from a dead hang with the shoulders packed and legs quiet.
Pull the chest toward the bar by driving the elbows down and slightly out.
Stop when the chin clears the bar or the chest nears the bar, then lower fully.
Avoid excessive kipping; use a band or machine assist if full reps are not yet available.

back-yates-row.name: Yates Row
instructions: Stand with a barbell using an underhand grip just outside the thighs, torso leaned forward about 30–45 degrees.
Brace the trunk and keep a soft knee bend with a flat back.
Row the bar to the lower abdomen by driving the elbows back along the sides.
Squeeze the lats at the top, then lower with control to straight arms.
Keep the torso angle steady; do not yank with the lower back.

cardio-assault-bike.name: Assault Bike
instructions: Adjust the Assault Bike seat so both feet reach the pedals with a slight knee bend at the bottom.
Place both feet on the pedals and both hands on the moving handles.
Drive the pedals and push-pull the handles together in a smooth cycle, building to the planned pace.
Keep the torso tall and the grip relaxed enough to avoid shrugged shoulders.
Ease the cadence before stopping so the fan resistance does not yank the arms.

cardio-burpee.name: Burpee
instructions: Stand tall on a clear floor with feet about hip-width and arms at the sides.
Drop the hands to the floor and jump or step both feet back into a plank.
Perform a push-up if desired, then jump or step the feet forward to the hands.
Explode upward into a jump with the arms reaching overhead, then land softly and repeat.
Step the feet instead of jumping if needed to keep the plank line and landing controlled.

cardio-cycling.name: Cycling
instructions: Set the bicycle saddle so the knee stays slightly bent at the bottom of the pedal stroke.
Clip in or place both feet securely on the pedals and hold the handlebars lightly.
Pedal with a smooth circular stroke, shifting gears to keep a sustainable cadence.
Keep the elbows soft and the eyes looking ahead along the route.
Obey traffic rules outdoors and stop if numbness or sharp knee pain appears.

cardio-elliptical.name: Elliptical
instructions: Step onto the elliptical pedals, hold the moving or fixed handles, and select a low resistance to start.
Stand tall with both feet fully on the pedals and a soft bend in the knees.
Drive the pedals through a smooth ellipse while the arms move naturally with the handles.
Keep the heels from lifting excessively and avoid leaning hard on the handles.
Increase incline or resistance only after the stride feels stable.

cardio-high-knees.name: High Knees
instructions: Stand tall on a clear floor with feet hip-width and arms bent at the sides as if running.
Brace the trunk and stay on the balls of the feet.
Drive one knee up toward hip height while the opposite arm swings forward, then switch legs rapidly.
Keep the torso upright and the landing light under the hips.
Slow the cadence if you start leaning back or slamming the feet.

cardio-incline-walk.name: Incline Walk
instructions: Set a treadmill to a walking speed and a moderate incline before stepping on the belt.
Place both feet on the side rails first, then step onto the moving belt holding the rails briefly if needed.
Walk with a natural stride, letting the arms swing freely once balanced.
Keep an upright posture and take shorter steps as the incline rises.
Reduce the incline to level before stopping and stepping off.

cardio-jump-rope.name: Jump Rope
instructions: Size the rope so the handles reach about the armpits when you stand on its center.
Hold a handle in each hand with elbows close to the ribs and jump on the balls of both feet.
Turn the rope with the wrists so it passes under the feet once per small bounce.
Keep jumps low and the torso quiet rather than tucking the knees high each time.
Clear enough ceiling and floor space, and stop if the rope repeatedly catches because of fatigue.

cardio-jumping-jack.name: Jumping Jack
instructions: Stand tall with feet together and arms at the sides on a clear non-slip floor.
Brace lightly through the trunk and soften the knees.
Jump the feet out wide while raising both arms out and overhead, then jump back to the start.
Land softly on the mid-foot and keep a steady rhythm.
Step the feet out instead of jumping if impact needs to be reduced.

cardio-rowing.name: Rowing
instructions: Sit in a rowing shell with both feet secured in the stretchers and the oars or sculls in hand.
Start at the catch with shins near vertical, arms reaching forward, and the torso slightly hinged.
Drive with the legs first, then swing the torso, then pull the handles to the body; recover in reverse order.
Keep the chain of motion smooth and avoid yanking early with the arms.
Follow local water safety rules and stop if balance or blade work becomes unsafe.

cardio-rowing-machine.name: Rowing Machine
instructions: Sit on the rower, strap both feet onto the footplates, and take the handle with an overhand grip.
Start at the catch with shins near vertical, arms long, and the torso hinged forward slightly.
Drive the legs, open the torso, then pull the handle to the lower ribs; recover arms, torso, then legs.
Keep the handle moving in a horizontal path and the shoulders relaxed.
Avoid rounding the lower back at the catch or yanking the chain with the arms first.

safety.title: Foot Straps
safety.description: Secure the footplates so the straps sit across the mid-foot—heels can lift slightly in the stroke.

safety.title: Damper / Resistance
safety.description: Start with a moderate damper; high settings are not required for a safe, effective stroke.

safety.title: Seat Path
safety.description: Ensure the seat rolls freely; keep shins near vertical at the catch without jamming the knees.

safety.title: Stroke Sequence
safety.description: Drive legs first, then hips, then arms; reverse on the recovery—avoid yanking with the back alone.

cardio-running.name: Running
instructions: Choose a clear route or track and start with an easy warm-up jog.
Stand tall with a slight forward lean from the ankles and relaxed shoulders.
Land under the hips with a quick, light cadence and let the arms swing opposite the legs.
Breathe rhythmically and keep the fists soft.
Slow to a walk if form collapses or unusual joint pain appears.

cardio-ski-erg.name: SkiErg
instructions: Stand facing the SkiErg with feet about hip-width and both hands on the handles overhead.
Brace the trunk and keep a soft bend in the knees.
Hinge and pull both handles down past the hips in a ski-poling motion, then return the hands overhead.
Drive with the lats and hips together rather than only bending the elbows early.
Keep the rope or cords from twisting and reset your stance if you start hopping.

cardio-spin-bike.name: Spin Bike
instructions: Adjust the spin-bike saddle and handlebars so both feet reach the pedals with a slight knee bend at the bottom.
Clip in or strap both shoes onto the pedals and hold the handlebars lightly.
Pedal a smooth circle, adding resistance with the bike's tension knob to match the planned effort.
Keep the hips stable on the saddle during seated work and the core braced when standing.
Reduce resistance before stopping and unclip carefully.

cardio-stair-climber.name: Stair Climber
instructions: Step onto the stair climber, hold the rails lightly, and select a slow starting speed.
Place the whole foot on each step and stand tall over the hips.
Climb with a steady alternating step, letting the arms move naturally or rest lightly on the rails.
Avoid locking the elbows on the rails and leaning the body weight into the hands.
Slow the machine fully before stepping off backward with care.

cardio-stairmaster.name: StairMaster
instructions: Step onto the StairMaster pedals, hold the rails lightly, and choose a manageable level.
Keep both feet fully on the pedals and the torso upright.
Press each pedal down in an alternating stepping pattern without letting the pedals slam.
Take short steps under the hips rather than overstriding.
Use the rails for balance only and stop if the knees cave inward repeatedly.

cardio-stationary-bike.name: Stationary Bike
instructions: Adjust the stationary-bike seat so both legs reach near full extension with a slight knee bend at the bottom.
Place both feet on the pedals or in the toe cages and hold the handlebars lightly.
Pedal at a steady cadence, adjusting resistance to match the planned effort.
Keep the hips level on the seat and the shoulders relaxed.
Reduce resistance before stopping and dismounting.

cardio-swimming.name: Swimming
instructions: Enter the pool at a safe depth and choose a clear lane for the planned stroke.
Push off the wall in a streamline with both arms extended and the body long.
Swim continuous strokes with rhythmic breathing to the planned side or pattern.
Kick from the hips with relaxed ankles and keep the head aligned with the spine.
Rest at the wall if technique breaks or you feel short of breath beyond training intent.

cardio-treadmill.name: Treadmill
instructions: Set the treadmill to a slow start speed, step on from the side rails, then gradually increase pace.
Stand tall with a slight forward lean and both feet landing under the hips.
Run or walk with a quick light cadence, letting the arms swing naturally.
Use the handrails only for brief balance checks, not continuous support.
Slow the belt fully before stepping onto the side rails to exit.

cardio-walking.name: Walking
instructions: Choose a clear, even route and stand tall with relaxed shoulders.
Place each foot heel-to-toe under the hips and let the arms swing opposite the legs.
Walk at a brisk sustainable pace while breathing comfortably through the nose and mouth.
Keep the gaze ahead and the stride smooth rather than overstriding.
Slow down on uneven surfaces and stop if unusual joint pain appears.

chest-bench-press.name: Bench Press
instructions: Lie on a flat bench under a racked barbell with eyes roughly under the bar.
Plant both feet on the floor, set the shoulder blades, and take an even overhand grip just outside shoulder width.
Unrack, lower the bar to mid-chest with elbows tracking under the wrists, then press back to straight arms.
Keep the hips on the bench and the bar path slightly diagonal toward the shoulders.
Use a spotter or safeties for heavy sets and never bounce the bar off the chest.

safety.title: J-Hook Height
safety.description: Set J-hooks so you can unrack with nearly locked elbows without shrugging the bar off the pins.

safety.title: Safety Arms
safety.description: Set safeties just below your lowest controlled chest position so they catch a failed rep.

safety.title: Foot Placement
safety.description: Plant both feet flat and wide enough to brace without lifting the hips off the bench.

safety.title: Shoulder Position
safety.description: Retract and depress the shoulder blades on the bench before unracking; keep them set through the set.

safety.title: Grip Width
safety.description: Choose a grip that keeps wrists stacked over elbows at the bottom—neither extreme flare nor crush.

safety.title: Spotter
safety.description: Use a spotter for heavy attempts, or keep safeties set if training alone.

chest-cable-crossover.name: Cable Crossover
instructions: Set both cable pulleys high and attach single handles, standing centered between the stacks.
Step forward into a staggered stance with a soft knee bend and a slight forward lean.
Start with arms open and elbows softly bent, palms facing forward or slightly down.
Sweep the handles down and together in front of the lower chest, squeeze, then return with control.
Keep the elbows fixed in their bend and stop before the shoulders shrug or overstretch behind the body.

safety.title: Pulley Height
safety.description: Set both pulleys high so the cables travel downward and inward across the chest.

safety.title: Handle Attachment
safety.description: Clip D-handles securely on both sides and confirm stack pins are fully seated.

safety.title: Starting Position
safety.description: Stand centered between stacks with a soft forward lean from the hips and a slight elbow bend.

safety.title: Body Alignment
safety.description: Keep ribs down and avoid overstretching the shoulders at the wide start position.

chest-cable-fly.name: Cable Fly
instructions: Set both cable pulleys around chest height and attach single handles.
Stand centered with a slight forward lean, feet staggered or even, and brace the trunk.
Hold the handles with elbows softly bent and palms facing each other.
Bring the hands together in a wide arc at chest level, pause, then open slowly to a comfortable stretch.
Do not lock the elbows or let the cable stacks slam between reps.

safety.title: Pulley Height
safety.description: Set pulleys at about mid-chest to upper-chest height for a horizontal fly path.

safety.title: Handle Attachment
safety.description: Clip D-handles securely on both sides and confirm stack pins are fully seated.

safety.title: Starting Position
safety.description: Step forward until there is light tension at the start with elbows soft and scapulae set.

safety.title: Body Alignment
safety.description: Keep ribs down and avoid overstretching the shoulders at the wide start position.

chest-chest-dip.name: Chest Dip
instructions: Grip parallel dip bars and support yourself on straight arms with the chest slightly forward.
Cross or keep the legs still and brace the abdomen before descending.
Bend the elbows and lower until the upper arms are near parallel, keeping the torso leaned forward.
Press back up to straight arms without locking out harshly.
Stop the set if the shoulders feel pinched or unstable at the bottom.

chest-decline-bench-press.name: Decline Bench Press
instructions: Secure your lower legs on a decline bench under a racked barbell.
Take an even overhand grip just outside shoulder width and set the shoulder blades against the pad.
Unrack, lower the bar to the lower chest, then press to straight arms.
Keep the wrists stacked over the elbows and the hips pressed into the bench.
Use safeties or a spotter and control the bar fully—decline angles make unracking less forgiving.

safety.title: Bench Angle
safety.description: Lock the decline securely and use the leg pads so you cannot slide toward the floor.

safety.title: Leg Support
safety.description: Hook the legs firmly under the pads before unracking; head stays supported on the bench.

safety.title: J-Hook Height
safety.description: Set hooks so the unrack is short and controlled on the decline path.

safety.title: Safety Arms
safety.description: Set safeties just below your lowest chest position on this decline angle.

safety.title: Spotter
safety.description: Prefer a spotter on heavy decline work; the bar path is harder to bail alone.

chest-decline-dumbbell-press.name: Decline Dumbbell Press
instructions: Secure your legs on a decline bench and hold a dumbbell in each hand at the lower chest.
Plant the shoulder blades, brace the trunk, and keep the wrists straight.
Press both dumbbells up until the arms are nearly straight, then lower them beside the lower chest.
Keep the elbows under the wrists and avoid letting the dumbbells drift toward the face.
Have a partner hand you the dumbbells if getting into position feels unstable.

safety.title: Bench Angle
safety.description: Lock the decline and secure the leg pads before lifting the dumbbells into place.

safety.title: Leg Support
safety.description: Hook both legs under the pads so you cannot slide; head stays on the bench.

safety.title: Shoulder Position
safety.description: Keep shoulder blades set on the pad; lower the bells to the lower chest with wrists stacked.

safety.title: Control
safety.description: Press with control—do not bounce dumbbells off the chest at the bottom.

chest-decline-push-up.name: Decline Push-Up
instructions: Place both feet on a stable bench or box and both hands on the floor under the shoulders.
Form a straight line from head to heels and brace the abdomen.
Lower the chest toward the floor by bending the elbows, then push back to the start.
Keep the hips level—do not pike or sag as the feet are elevated.
Choose a lower surface if you cannot keep the shoulders stable through the full range.

chest-diamond-push-up.name: Diamond Push-Up
instructions: Place both hands on the floor so the thumbs and index fingers form a diamond under the chest.
Extend the legs into a plank with the body in one straight line.
Lower the chest toward the hands by bending the elbows close to the ribs, then press up.
Keep the elbows tracking back rather than flaring wide.
Drop to the knees only if needed, still keeping the diamond hand position.

chest-dumbbell-bench-press.name: Dumbbell Bench Press
instructions: Lie on a flat bench holding a dumbbell in each hand at the outer edges of the chest.
Plant both feet, set the shoulder blades, and keep the wrists straight above the elbows.
Press the dumbbells up until the arms are nearly straight, then lower them to the start.
Keep the dumbbells moving together without clanking them hard at the top.
Control the descent—unstable shoulders or open elbows mean the load is too heavy.

safety.title: Bench Stability
safety.description: Confirm the flat bench is locked and stable before kicking the dumbbells into position.

safety.title: Foot Placement
safety.description: Plant both feet flat; do not lift the hips to finish heavy presses.

safety.title: Shoulder Position
safety.description: Retract the shoulder blades on the bench and keep dumbbells stacked over the wrists and elbows.

safety.title: Starting Position
safety.description: Bring dumbbells to the chest with control; avoid dropping them from an overhead kick-up.

chest-dumbbell-fly.name: Dumbbell Fly
instructions: Lie on a flat bench with a dumbbell in each hand pressed above the chest, palms facing each other.
Keep a soft fixed bend in both elbows and plant the feet on the floor.
Open the arms in a wide arc until the chest is comfortably stretched, then bring the dumbbells back together.
Keep the movement in the shoulder joint—do not turn it into a press by bending the elbows more.
Use a range that never forces the upper arms behind a painful stretch.

chest-dumbbell-pullover.name: Dumbbell Pullover
instructions: Lie across or along a flat bench holding one dumbbell above the chest with both hands under the upper plate.
Keep a soft elbow bend and brace the trunk so the ribs stay down.
Lower the dumbbell in an arc behind the head until you feel a stretch through the chest and lats.
Pull the weight back over the chest by driving the elbows toward the hips.
Move slowly through the bottom and stop if the shoulders feel pinched.

chest-floor-press.name: Floor Press
instructions: Lie on the floor with knees bent and a barbell or dumbbells held above the chest.
Set the shoulder blades into the floor and take a grip just outside shoulder width.
Lower the weight until the upper arms lightly touch the floor, keeping elbows under the wrists.
Press back to straight arms without bouncing the arms off the floor.
Keep the ribs down and stop the set if the elbows crash into the floor.

chest-incline-bench-press.name: Incline Bench Press
instructions: Set an adjustable bench to a moderate incline under a racked barbell.
Lie back with feet planted, take an even overhand grip, and set the shoulder blades.
Unrack and lower the bar to the upper chest, then press to straight arms.
Keep the wrists stacked over the elbows and avoid flaring them excessively.
Choose an incline of about 30–45°—steeper angles shift the work toward the shoulders.

safety.title: Bench Angle
safety.description: Lock the bench at a moderate incline so the bar path stays over the upper chest—not the neck.

safety.title: J-Hook Height
safety.description: Set J-hooks for a short, controlled unrack on the incline path.

safety.title: Safety Arms
safety.description: Set safeties just below your lowest upper-chest position on this incline.

safety.title: Foot Placement
safety.description: Plant both feet; hips stay on the pad—do not bridge off the incline.

safety.title: Shoulder Position
safety.description: Set scapulae back and down before unracking; keep the bar over the upper chest.

chest-incline-dumbbell-fly.name: Incline Dumbbell Fly
instructions: Set a bench to a moderate incline and hold a dumbbell in each hand above the upper chest.
Keep a soft fixed elbow bend, feet planted, and shoulder blades on the pad.
Open the arms in a wide arc, then squeeze the dumbbells back together above the chest.
Keep the palms facing each other and the wrists neutral throughout.
Stop the stretch before the shoulders feel forced or pinched on the incline.

safety.title: Bench Angle
safety.description: Lock a mild-to-moderate incline; a steep incline turns the fly into an awkward shoulder raise.

safety.title: Shoulder Position
safety.description: Keep a soft bend in the elbows and scapulae set—do not let the bells pull the shoulders forward.

safety.title: Range of Motion
safety.description: Lower only until you feel a chest stretch; stop before the shoulders feel unstable.

safety.title: Foot Placement
safety.description: Plant both feet for a stable base on the incline pad.

chest-incline-dumbbell-press.name: Incline Dumbbell Press
instructions: Set a bench to a moderate incline and hold dumbbells at the outer upper chest.
Plant the feet, brace the trunk, and keep the wrists stacked over the elbows.
Press both dumbbells up and slightly together, then lower them to the start.
Keep the elbows under the wrists rather than letting them drop behind the bench line.
Have help positioning heavy dumbbells so the first rep starts from a stable set position.

safety.title: Bench Angle
safety.description: Lock a moderate incline so the presses load the upper chest without the bells drifting over the face.

safety.title: Foot Placement
safety.description: Keep both feet planted; hips stay on the pad throughout the set.

safety.title: Shoulder Position
safety.description: Set the scapulae back and down before the first press; elbows track under the weights.

safety.title: Range of Motion
safety.description: Lower until upper arms are about parallel to the floor or slightly below—stop if shoulders pinch.

chest-incline-machine-press.name: Incline Machine Press
instructions: Adjust the incline press seat so the handles align with the upper chest when you sit back.
Place both feet on the floor or footplate and grip the handles evenly.
Press the handles forward until the arms are nearly straight, then return with control.
Keep the head and upper back against the pad throughout the set.
Select a seat height that does not force the shoulders into a shrugged start.

safety.title: Seat Height
safety.description: Set the seat so handles meet the upper chest or lower shoulder line when you sit against the pad.

safety.title: Back Support
safety.description: Keep the back and head glued to the pad so the press stays on the upper chest path.

safety.title: Handle Starting Position
safety.description: Handles should begin just in front of the upper chest—not beside the ears.

safety.title: Foot Placement
safety.description: Plant both feet; do not lift the hips to finish the last reps.

chest-incline-push-up.name: Incline Push-Up
instructions: Place both hands on a stable bench, bar, or box and both feet on the floor behind you.
Form a straight body line from head to heels with the hands under the shoulders.
Lower the chest toward the surface by bending the elbows, then press back up.
Keep the elbows about 45° from the torso and the neck long.
Raise the hand surface if the floor version is too hard; lower it as you get stronger.

chest-landmine-press.name: Landmine Press
instructions: Secure one end of a barbell in a landmine base and hold the free end at the front of one shoulder.
Stand in a staggered stance with the same-side foot back and the trunk braced.
Press the bar up and slightly forward along the landmine arc, then lower to the shoulder.
Keep the elbow under the hand and the ribs stacked over the pelvis.
Finish the planned reps, then switch shoulders and stance for the other side.

chest-low-to-high-cable-fly.name: Low-to-High Cable Fly
instructions: Set both cable pulleys low and attach single handles, standing centered between the stacks.
Step forward into a staggered stance with a slight forward lean and soft elbows.
Start with hands near the hips, palms facing forward or slightly up.
Sweep the handles up and together to about upper-chest or chin height, then lower with control.
Keep the elbow bend fixed and avoid shrugging the shoulders at the top.

safety.title: Pulley Height
safety.description: Set both pulleys low so the cables travel upward and inward toward the upper chest.

safety.title: Handle Attachment
safety.description: Clip D-handles securely on both sides and confirm stack pins are fully seated.

safety.title: Starting Position
safety.description: Stand tall between stacks; raise handles on an upward arc without shrugging the traps.

safety.title: Body Alignment
safety.description: Keep ribs down and avoid overstretching the shoulders at the wide start position.

chest-machine-chest-press.name: Machine Chest Press
instructions: Adjust the seat so the machine handles sit at mid-chest when your back is against the pad.
Place both feet firmly and grip the handles with even hand spacing.
Press the handles forward until the arms are nearly straight, then return slowly.
Keep the shoulder blades against the pad and avoid shrugging into the finish.
Choose a grip height that lets the elbows stay roughly under the wrists.

safety.title: Seat Height
safety.description: Adjust the seat so the handles align with mid-chest when you sit tall against the back pad.

safety.title: Backrest Position
safety.description: Keep your back and head against the pad; do not round forward to reach the handles.

safety.title: Handle Alignment
safety.description: Handles should start at mid-chest; incorrect height can overload the shoulders.

safety.title: Foot Placement
safety.description: Plant both feet flat before pressing; keep hips on the seat through the set.

safety.title: Starting Range
safety.description: Begin with elbows roughly in line with the torso—avoid an extreme stretch behind the body.

chest-pec-deck.name: Pec Deck
instructions: Adjust the pec-deck seat so the pads or handles align with mid-chest and your elbows are slightly below shoulder height.
Sit tall with the back against the pad and forearms on the pads or hands on the grips.
Bring the arms together in front of the chest, squeeze, then open to a controlled stretch.
Keep the elbows at a fixed soft bend if using handles.
Do not let the weight stack yank the arms behind the torso at the start of each rep.

safety.title: Seat Height
safety.description: Adjust the seat so your elbows stay level with the arm pads at mid-chest height.

safety.title: Arm Pad Adjustment
safety.description: Set arm pads so forearms rest fully on them with elbows slightly bent—not jammed into the shoulders.

safety.title: Elbow Position
safety.description: Keep elbows on the pads and roughly in line with mid-chest; avoid lifting elbows above the shoulders.

safety.title: Back Support
safety.description: Sit tall against the back pad; do not shrug or lean forward to close the pads.

chest-push-up.name: Push-Up
instructions: Place both hands on the floor roughly under the shoulders and extend the legs into a plank.
Brace the abdomen and squeeze the glutes so the body forms one straight line.
Lower the chest toward the floor by bending the elbows, then push the floor away to full extension.
Keep the elbows about 30–45° from the torso rather than flared to 90°.
Do not let the hips sag or pike; shorten the range or elevate the hands if form breaks.

chest-smith-incline-press.name: Smith Machine Incline Press
instructions: Set an adjustable bench to a moderate incline inside a Smith machine and lie with eyes under the bar.
Unrack with an even overhand grip just outside shoulder width and plant both feet.
Lower the bar to the upper chest with elbows tracking under the wrists.
Press along the fixed path to straight arms without locking out aggressively.
Keep the hips on the bench and use the safeties if training heavy alone.

safety.title: Bench Angle
safety.description: Lock a moderate incline under the Smith bar so the path meets the upper chest.

safety.title: Safety Stops
safety.description: Set stops just below the lowest controlled position on this incline before loading.

safety.title: Bar Starting Height
safety.description: Unhook with nearly locked elbows; avoid reaching high to free the bar.

safety.title: Foot Placement
safety.description: Plant both feet; keep hips on the incline pad throughout the set.

chest-smith-machine-bench-press.name: Smith Machine Bench Press
instructions: Set a flat bench under the Smith bar and adjust the safety stops to just below chest level.
Lie with eyes under the bar, plant the feet, and take an even overhand grip.
Rotate the bar to unlock, lower it to mid-chest, then press to straight arms and re-rack.
Keep the shoulder blades set and the hips on the bench throughout.
Because the bar path is fixed, stop if the forced line irritates the shoulders.

safety.title: Safety Stops
safety.description: Set Smith safety stops just below your lowest controlled chest position before loading the bar.

safety.title: Bar Starting Height
safety.description: Begin with the bar at a height that lets you unhook with nearly locked elbows.

safety.title: Foot Placement
safety.description: Plant both feet flat; hips stay on the bench—do not bridge excessively under the fixed bar path.

safety.title: Shoulder Position
safety.description: Set scapulae back and down; keep wrists stacked as the bar travels on the Smith rails.

chest-squeeze-press.name: Squeeze Press
instructions: Lie on a flat bench holding two dumbbells pressed together above the chest, palms facing each other.
Keep constant inward pressure so the dumbbells stay touching through the set.
Lower the weights to mid-chest with elbows tracking under the wrists.
Press back up while continuing to squeeze the dumbbells together.
Use a controlled tempo; do not let the bells separate or bounce off the chest.

chest-svend-press.name: Svend Press
instructions: Stand or sit tall and squeeze one or two light plates together between both palms at chest height.
Keep the elbows out to the sides and the plates pressed tightly so they do not slip.
Press the plates straight forward until the arms are nearly straight, then return to the chest.
Maintain constant inward pressure between the palms for the whole set.
Use smooth plates you can grip securely; stop if they start to separate.

chest-weighted-chest-dip.name: Weighted Chest Dip
instructions: Secure a dip belt with plates or hold a dumbbell between the feet, then grip parallel bars.
Support yourself on straight arms with the chest leaned slightly forward.
Lower until the upper arms approach parallel, then press back to the top.
Keep the load hanging still so it does not swing and pull you forward.
Start lighter than bodyweight dips—added load amplifies shoulder stress at the bottom.

core-ab-wheel.name: Ab Wheel
instructions: Kneel on a pad holding the ab-wheel handles with the wheel directly under the shoulders.
Brace the abdomen hard and keep the hips slightly tucked.
Roll the wheel forward while the arms stay nearly straight, then pull it back to the start by flexing the trunk.
Keep the ribs and pelvis connected—do not let the lower back sag toward the floor.
Shorten the roll-out until you can control the full range without collapsing.

core-bicycle-crunch.name: Bicycle Crunch
instructions: Lie on your back on a mat with hands lightly behind the head and both knees bent toward the chest.
Lift the shoulder blades off the floor and keep the lower back pressed down.
Extend one leg while rotating the opposite elbow toward the bent knee, then switch sides in a pedaling rhythm.
Rotate from the torso rather than yanking the head with the hands.
Keep both shoulder blades hovering and avoid fully resting between switches.

core-bird-dog.name: Bird Dog
instructions: Start on all fours with hands under the shoulders and knees under the hips.
Brace the abdomen so the spine stays neutral like a tabletop.
Extend one arm forward and the opposite leg back until both are in line with the torso, pause, then return.
Keep the hips level—do not rotate or sag as the limbs lift.
Alternate sides each rep, moving slowly enough to keep balance.

core-cable-crunch.name: Cable Crunch
instructions: Attach a rope to the high pulley, kneel facing the stack, and hold the rope ends beside the head.
Keep the hips still over the knees and brace before moving.
Curl the ribs toward the pelvis by flexing the spine, pause, then return without standing up.
Pull with the abdominals rather than yanking the rope with the arms.
Keep the cable taut at the top so the stack does not slam.

safety.title: Pulley Height
safety.description: Set the pulley high so the rope hangs above the head before you kneel or stand.

safety.title: Handle Attachment
safety.description: Clip a rope securely; hold both ends beside the head without yanking the neck.

safety.title: Knee / Stance Position
safety.description: Kneel or stand far enough that tension exists at the start without the stack slamming.

safety.title: Spine Motion
safety.description: Crunch by flexing the spine; do not yank with the arms or hyperextend the neck.

core-captains-chair-knee-raise.name: Captain's Chair Knee Raise
instructions: Step onto a captain's chair, press the forearms into the pads, and let the legs hang straight.
Press the back against the support and brace the abdomen.
Lift both knees toward the chest by curling the pelvis, then lower without swinging.
Avoid shrugging into the arm pads or using momentum from the legs.
Stop the set if the lower back peels hard off the pad.

safety.title: Arm / Elbow Pads
safety.description: Rest the forearms fully on the pads with shoulders packed down—not shrugged into the ears.

safety.title: Back Support
safety.description: Keep the back against the pad; avoid swinging away from the chair.

safety.title: Grip Handles
safety.description: Hold the handles only for stability; do not shrug up through the traps to lift the knees.

safety.title: Controlled Motion
safety.description: Raise the knees with control; avoid excessive swinging that stresses the low back.

core-crunch.name: Crunch
instructions: Lie on your back on a mat with knees bent, feet flat, and hands lightly behind the head or across the chest.
Press the lower back into the floor and brace before lifting.
Curl the shoulder blades off the floor toward the pelvis, pause, then lower slowly.
Keep the neck long—do not pull the head forward with the hands.
Move through a short spinal flexion range rather than sitting all the way up.

core-dead-bug.name: Dead Bug
instructions: Lie on your back with both arms reaching toward the ceiling and both hips and knees bent to 90°.
Press the lower back into the floor and brace the abdomen.
Extend one arm overhead and the opposite leg toward the floor, then return and switch sides.
Keep the ribs down so the lower back never arches off the mat.
Move slowly enough that the pelvis stays still throughout.

core-decline-sit-up.name: Decline Sit-Up
instructions: Secure both lower legs under the pads of a decline bench and lie back with the torso supported.
Place the hands across the chest or lightly behind the head and brace.
Curl the torso up toward the thighs, then lower under control to the bench.
Lead with the ribs rather than yanking the neck forward.
Use a shallower decline if you cannot control the eccentric.

core-dragon-flag.name: Dragon Flag
instructions: Lie on a bench and grip the bench or a stable support behind the head with both hands.
Brace hard and lift the legs and hips so the body forms a straight line from shoulders to toes.
Lower the rigid body toward the bench under control, then raise it back without bending at the hips.
Keep the body as one piece—do not pike or sag through the lower back.
Use a tucked or single-leg progression until the full lever is controllable.

core-hanging-knee-raise.name: Hanging Knee Raise
instructions: Hang from a pull-up bar with an overhand grip and arms straight.
Set the shoulders slightly down and brace the trunk before moving the legs.
Lift both knees toward the chest by curling the pelvis, then lower without swinging.
Avoid kipping or using a large body swing to raise the knees.
Stop short if the grip or shoulders lose their active hang position.

core-hanging-leg-raise.name: Hanging Leg Raise
instructions: Hang from a pull-up bar with arms straight and legs together.
Brace the trunk and keep a slight active shoulder set.
Raise both straight legs toward horizontal or higher by curling the pelvis, then lower slowly.
Minimize swing—pause briefly at the bottom if needed to still the body.
