Bend the knees only as a regression if straight legs break your form.

core-hanging-windshield-wiper.name: Hanging Windshield Wiper
instructions: Hang from a pull-up bar, raise both legs until they are near vertical, and keep the arms straight.
Brace the trunk hard and keep the shoulders active.
Rotate both legs together from side to side like a windshield wiper while the hips stay high.
Control each side equally and avoid yanking from the lower back.
Reduce the range or keep the knees bent until you can move without swinging wildly.

core-hollow-hold.name: Hollow Hold
instructions: Lie on your back and press the lower back into the mat with arms reaching overhead and legs extended.
Lift the shoulders and legs just off the floor into a shallow banana shape.
Hold that hollow position while breathing steadily into the braced abdomen.
Keep the ribs down so the lower back never arches off the floor.
Bend the knees or lower the arms to the sides if you cannot keep the back flat.

core-leg-raise.name: Lying Leg Raise
instructions: Lie on your back on a mat with legs extended and hands under the hips or at the sides for support.
Press the lower back into the floor and brace before lifting.
Raise both legs toward vertical, then lower them slowly without letting the back arch.
Keep the legs as straight as hamstring mobility allows.
Stop the descent before the lower back peels up if control is lost.

core-machine-crunch.name: Machine Crunch
instructions: Adjust the crunch-machine seat and pads so the chest pad sits on the upper chest and the pivot matches your midsection.
Secure the legs if required and hold the handles without yanking.
Curl the torso forward against the pad, pause, then return slowly.
Flex the spine rather than pulling only with the arms.
Select a load that lets you feel the abdominals without using momentum.

safety.title: Seat Height
safety.description: Sit so the pivot aligns roughly with the mid-torso and pads meet the upper chest or shoulders.

safety.title: Pad Position
safety.description: Rest the chest or shoulder pads evenly; avoid putting pressure on the throat.

safety.title: Foot Support
safety.description: Secure feet or ankles as the machine provides so the hips stay down.

safety.title: Range of Motion
safety.description: Crunch through a controlled range; do not slam the weight stack at either end.

core-mountain-climber.name: Mountain Climber
instructions: Start in a high plank with both hands under the shoulders and the body in one straight line.
Brace the abdomen and keep the hips level.
Drive one knee toward the chest, then quickly switch legs in a running rhythm while the hands stay planted.
Keep the shoulders over the hands and avoid bouncing the hips up and down.
Slow the cadence if the plank line collapses or the hands slide.

core-pallof-press.name: Pallof Press
instructions: Attach a single handle at chest height and stand side-on to the cable with the handle held at the sternum.
Set a athletic stance with feet about hip-width and the trunk braced against the cable's pull.
Press both hands straight forward until the arms are extended, pause, then return to the chest.
Resist rotating toward or away from the stack—the torso should stay square.
Finish the reps facing one way, then turn around so the cable pulls from the other side.

core-plank.name: Plank
instructions: Place both forearms on the floor with elbows under the shoulders and extend the legs into a straight line.
Brace the abdomen and squeeze the glutes so the hips neither sag nor pike.
Hold the position while breathing steadily, keeping the neck long.
Press the forearms down and keep the shoulders away from the ears.
End the hold when the form breaks rather than accumulating sagging time.

core-russian-twist.name: Russian Twist
instructions: Sit on the floor with knees bent, feet on the floor or hovering, and lean the torso back to a balanced V shape.
Clasp the hands together in front of the chest and brace the trunk.
Rotate the torso to tap the hands toward the floor beside one hip, then to the other side.
Keep the chest up and move from the midsection rather than only swinging the arms.
Touch both sides for one rep and slow down if the feet slam or the back rounds hard.

core-side-plank.name: Side Plank
instructions: Lie on one side with the elbow under the shoulder and the legs stacked, then lift the hips into a straight line.
Support on the forearm and the side of the bottom foot, stacking the shoulders.
Hold the side plank while bracing the underside of the waist and breathing steadily.
Keep the hips forward—do not let them sag back or droop to the floor.
Switch to the other side after the timed hold and match the elbow and foot placement.

core-sit-up.name: Sit-Up
instructions: Lie on your back with knees bent, feet flat, and arms crossed on the chest or reaching forward.
Brace the abdomen and keep the feet lightly anchored if needed.
Curl the torso all the way up to a seated position, then lower under control to the floor.
Lead with the chest rather than yanking the neck forward.
Reduce the range to a crunch if the hip flexors take over or the back feels strained.

core-toes-to-bar.name: Toes-to-Bar
instructions: Hang from a pull-up bar with an overhand grip and arms straight.
Start with a hollow body position and active shoulders.
Raise both straight legs until the toes touch the bar, then lower under control.
Use a kip only if that is your intended style; otherwise keep the swing small and controlled.
Bend the knees as a progression if straight-leg contact is not yet possible.

core-v-up.name: V-Up
instructions: Lie flat on your back on a mat with arms extended overhead and legs straight.
Brace the abdomen and press the lower back toward the floor to start.
Simultaneously lift the legs and torso so the hands reach toward the toes into a V shape, then lower with control.
Keep the legs as straight as possible and avoid using a wild rock for momentum.
Touch the shins instead of the toes if hamstring mobility limits the full reach.

core-weighted-russian-twist.name: Weighted Russian Twist
instructions: Sit on the floor holding a plate, medicine ball, or dumbbell at the chest, knees bent, and lean back into a balanced V shape.
Keep both feet on the floor or hovering together and brace the trunk around the weight.
Rotate the torso to move the weight beside one hip, then to the other side under control.
Keep the load close to the body and the chest up rather than rounding hard through the back.
Touch both sides for one rep and slow down if the weight pulls you off balance.

core-woodchop.name: Cable Woodchop
instructions: Attach a single handle high or low on a cable stack and stand side-on with feet wider than the hips.
Hold the handle with both hands and brace the trunk over soft knees.
Rotate the torso to pull the handle across the body from high to low or low to high, then return under control.
Keep the arms fairly straight and turn through the midsection, not just the arms.
Finish the reps facing one way, then switch sides so the cable approaches from the opposite side.

safety.title: Pulley Height
safety.description: Set the pulley high for high-to-low chops or low for low-to-high, matching your planned path.

safety.title: Handle Attachment
safety.description: Clip a D-handle or rope securely before loading the stack.

safety.title: Stance & Distance
safety.description: Take a stable athletic stance far enough that the cable stays clear of the body through the rotate.

safety.title: Body Alignment
safety.description: Rotate through the torso with soft knees; do not wrench the low back with locked hips.

legs-back-squat.name: Back Squat
instructions: Set a barbell on a squat rack at about upper-chest height and step under it so it sits on the upper back.
Unrack with feet about shoulder-width, toes slightly out, and brace the trunk hard.
Sit the hips down and back until the thighs reach at least parallel, then drive through the mid-foot to stand.
Keep the knees tracking over the toes and the chest roughly over the mid-foot.
Re-rack carefully between sets and never bounce out of a collapsed bottom position.

legs-belt-squat.name: Belt Squat
instructions: Secure the belt around the hips and attach it to the belt-squat machine or loading pin.
Stand on the platform with feet about shoulder-width and hold the handles only for balance.
Sit the hips down into a squat, then drive through the feet to stand without leaning hard on the hands.
Keep the torso more upright than a back squat since the load hangs from the hips.
Unlock the carriage fully before loading the descent, and re-rack before exiting the belt.

legs-bodyweight-squat.name: Bodyweight Squat
instructions: Stand with feet about shoulder-width and toes slightly turned out on a clear floor.
Brace the abdomen and extend the arms forward or place the hands on the hips.
Sit the hips down and back to a controlled depth, then stand by pushing through the whole foot.
Keep the heels down and the knees tracking in the direction of the toes.
Pause at the bottom if balance is lost, rather than bouncing out of the hole.

legs-bulgarian-split-squat.name: Bulgarian Split Squat
instructions: Stand in a split stance with the rear foot elevated on a stable bench and the front foot far enough forward for balance.
Hold dumbbells at the sides or a barbell on the back, and brace the trunk over the front leg.
Lower the rear knee toward the floor by bending the front knee, then drive through the front foot to rise.
Keep most of the weight on the front leg and the front heel grounded.
Finish the reps on one leg, then switch the elevated foot and match the stance on the other side.

legs-cable-pull-through.name: Cable Pull-Through
instructions: Attach a rope to the low pulley, face away from the stack, and straddle the cable so it passes between the legs.
Hold both rope ends at the hips with arms straight and hinge the torso forward with a soft knee bend.
Drive the hips forward to stand tall until the glutes squeeze, then hinge back to the start.
Keep the arms passive—the hips, not the arms, should move the rope.
Maintain a neutral spine and stop if the lower back rounds to reach farther.

safety.title: Pulley Height
safety.description: Set the pulley low so the cable travels between the legs from the floor stack.

safety.title: Handle Attachment
safety.description: Clip a rope securely; face away from the stack and straddle the cable.

safety.title: Distance from Machine
safety.description: Step forward until there is tension at the start of the hinge without yanking the stack.

safety.title: Body Alignment
safety.description: Hinge with a neutral spine; drive the hips through without hyperextending the low back at lockout.

legs-donkey-calf-raise.name: Donkey Calf Raise
instructions: Set up on a donkey calf machine or bend forward with hips supported and the balls of both feet on a raised edge.
Let the heels hang below the platform and place any load on the hips as the machine requires.
Raise both heels as high as possible, pause, then lower into a full stretch.
Keep the knees softly straight without locking them hard.
Avoid bouncing at the bottom or rolling the ankles outward.

safety.title: Hip Pad
safety.description: Set the pad across the hips so load sits on the pelvis, not the low back.

safety.title: Foot Placement
safety.description: Place the balls of the feet on the block with heels free to move through a full range.

safety.title: Torso Support
safety.description: Brace the torso on the machine pads as designed; keep the neck neutral.

safety.title: Control
safety.description: Avoid bouncing out of the bottom stretch under the hip pad.

legs-dumbbell-lunge.name: Dumbbell Lunge
instructions: Stand tall holding a dumbbell in each hand at the sides with feet together.
Step one foot forward into a long enough stride that both knees can bend about 90°.
Lower the back knee toward the floor, then push through the front foot to return to standing.
Keep the trunk upright and the front knee tracking over the toes.
Alternate legs each rep or finish one side first, then switch, keeping the dumbbells still at the sides.

legs-dumbbell-romanian-deadlift.name: Dumbbell Romanian Deadlift
instructions: Stand holding a dumbbell in each hand in front of the thighs with a soft knee bend.
Brace the trunk and keep the shoulder blades gently set.
Push the hips back to slide the dumbbells down the thighs until you feel a hamstring stretch, then drive the hips forward to stand.
Keep the dumbbells close to the legs and the spine long.
Stop the descent before the lower back rounds or the dumbbells drift away from the body.

legs-front-squat.name: Front Squat
instructions: Set a barbell in a front-rack across the fronts of the shoulders with elbows high, or use a cross-arm rack.
Unrack with feet about shoulder-width and brace the trunk upright.
Sit straight down between the hips to depth, then drive through the mid-foot to stand.
Keep the elbows up so the bar stays on the shoulders rather than rolling forward.
Use a lighter load than the back squat if the rack position limits depth or balance.

legs-glute-bridge.name: Glute Bridge
instructions: Lie on your back on a mat with knees bent, feet flat about hip-width, and arms by the sides.
Brace the abdomen and tuck the pelvis slightly so the lower back is not arched hard.
Drive through the heels to raise the hips until the body forms a line from shoulders to knees, then lower slowly.
Squeeze the glutes at the top without pushing the ribs toward the ceiling.
Keep both feet planted and avoid shifting to one side.

legs-glute-ham-raise.name: Glute-Ham Raise
instructions: Adjust the GHD so the thighs rest on the pad and the ankles are secured under the foot rollers.
Start with the body roughly in a straight line from knees to head and brace the trunk.
Lower the torso by extending at the knees under control, then curl back up by flexing the knees and hips.
Keep the hips extended as you rise so the hamstrings, not a back arch, do the work.
Use a shortened range or band assistance until you can control the full eccentric.

legs-glute-kickback.name: Glute Kickback
instructions: Attach an ankle cuff to the low cable or set a glute-kickback machine pad against one foot.
Face the support, hold the frame, and keep the working leg's hip square with a soft bend in the standing knee.
Drive the working leg straight back from the hip, squeeze the glute, then return without touching down hard.
Keep the torso still—do not arch the lower back to lift the leg higher.
Finish the reps on one leg, then switch the cuff or pad to the other ankle.

legs-goblet-squat.name: Goblet Squat
instructions: Hold one dumbbell or kettlebell vertically against the center of the chest with both hands.
Stand with feet about shoulder-width and toes slightly out, elbows pointing down beside the weight.
Sit the hips down between the knees to depth, then drive through the mid-foot to stand.
Keep the chest tall and the weight glued to the sternum throughout.
Use the elbows to gently track inside the knees without forcing an extreme stance.

legs-hack-squat.name: Hack Squat
instructions: Step onto the hack-squat platform, place the shoulders under the pads, and set both feet about shoulder-width.
Unlock the safeties, brace the trunk, and keep the back flat against the pad.
Bend the knees to lower into a squat, then press the platform away to stand.
Keep the heels down and the knees tracking over the toes.
Re-engage the safeties before stepping out, and stop if the lower back peels off the pad.

safety.title: Shoulder Pads
safety.description: Set shoulder pads so they sit comfortably without forcing the neck into flexion.

safety.title: Foot Placement
safety.description: Place feet mid-platform at a stance that lets knees track over the toes.

safety.title: Back Position
safety.description: Keep the back and hips against the sled pad throughout the squat.

safety.title: Safe Depth
safety.description: Descend to a depth you can control without the low back peeling off the pad.

safety.title: Safety Stops
safety.description: Engage safety catches if the machine provides them before loading heavy.

legs-hip-abduction.name: Hip Abduction
instructions: Sit in the hip-abduction machine with the outer thigh pads just above the knees and the back against the pad.
Hold the handles and place both feet on the footrests.
Open both knees outward against the pads, pause, then return slowly without letting the stack slam.
Keep the pelvis still on the seat—do not rock to gain range.
Use a pain-free range; sharp hip pinching means the seat or start angle needs adjustment.

legs-hip-adduction.name: Hip Adduction
instructions: Sit in the hip-adduction machine with the inner thigh pads just above the knees and the back against the pad.
Hold the handles and place both feet on the footrests with the legs open to the start angle.
Squeeze both knees inward against the pads, pause, then return slowly to the open position.
Keep the torso upright and the pelvis planted on the seat.
Do not bounce the pads together or force a range that pinches the groin.

legs-hip-thrust.name: Hip Thrust
instructions: Sit on the floor with the upper back against a stable bench and a padded barbell over the hip crease.
Plant both feet flat about hip-width so the shins will be nearly vertical at the top.
Drive through the heels to raise the hips until the torso is roughly parallel to the floor, then lower with control.
Tuck the chin slightly and finish by squeezing the glutes, not by overarching the lower back.
Keep both feet grounded and the bar centered over the hips for the whole set.

legs-lateral-lunge.name: Lateral Lunge
instructions: Stand tall with feet together, holding dumbbells at the sides or with hands free.
Take a wide step directly to one side and keep the trailing leg straight.
Sit the hips back and down over the stepping leg, then push off that foot to return to center.
Keep the stepping foot flat and the knee tracking over the toes.
Alternate sides each rep or finish one side, then switch, without letting the torso collapse forward.

legs-leg-extension.name: Leg Extension
instructions: Adjust the seat so the knees align with the machine's pivot and the shin pad rests just above the ankles.
Sit back against the pad, hold the handles, and place both shins behind the pad.
Extend both knees until the legs are nearly straight, pause, then lower slowly.
Keep the hips down on the seat for the entire set.
Avoid slamming into a hard lockout or using momentum from the torso.

safety.title: Backrest Adjustment
safety.description: Sit with the back against the pad so the thighs are fully supported on the seat.

safety.title: Knee Alignment
safety.description: Adjust the seat so your knees align with the machine’s pivot point.

safety.title: Roller Pad
safety.description: Place the roller pad just above the ankles—not on the shin bone mid-tibia.

safety.title: Seat Depth
safety.description: Move the seat forward or back so the back of the knees clears the seat edge comfortably.

legs-leg-press.name: Leg Press
instructions: Sit in the leg-press seat, place both feet about shoulder-width on the platform, and adjust the seat so the knees start comfortably bent.
Grip the handles, brace the trunk, and unlock the sled safeties.
Bend both knees to lower the platform toward the chest, then press through the whole foot to extend the legs.
Keep the lower back and hips glued to the seat—do not let the pelvis curl under at the bottom.
Re-rack the safeties before exiting and stop short of locking the knees violently.

safety.title: Seat / Back Angle
safety.description: Adjust the seat or sled so your back and sacrum stay flat on the pad through the press.

safety.title: Foot Placement
safety.description: Place feet mid-platform, about hip- to shoulder-width, with heels planted—not only on the toes.

safety.title: Knee Tracking
safety.description: Knees track over the toes; do not let them collapse hard inward under load.

safety.title: Safe Depth
safety.description: Lower until thighs approach a right angle or your comfortable depth without the low back rounding off the pad.

safety.title: Lockout Warning
safety.description: Finish with soft knees—do not slam the joints into a hard lockout under heavy load.

legs-leg-press-calf-raise.name: Leg Press Calf Raise
instructions: Sit in the leg press and place only the balls of both feet on the lower edge of the platform with heels hanging off.
Press the platform to a soft knee extension and hold that leg position steady.
Lower both heels for a stretch, then rise onto the balls of the feet as high as possible and pause.
Keep the ankles aligned and avoid letting the knees bend and straighten each rep.
Use a controlled range and keep the safeties set before starting.

safety.title: Foot Placement
safety.description: Place the balls of the feet on the lower edge of the platform with heels free to move.

safety.title: Seat Position
safety.description: Keep the back against the pad with soft knees—do not fully lock the legs while raising the heels.

safety.title: Range of Motion
safety.description: Lower heels under control; stop if you feel a sharp pull in the Achilles or calf.

safety.title: Safety
safety.description: Use a light enough load that you can control the stretch—do not bounce out of the bottom.

legs-lying-leg-curl.name: Lying Leg Curl
instructions: Lie face-down on the lying leg-curl machine with the knee joint aligned to the pivot and the roller just above the heels.
Grip the handles and keep the hips pressed into the pad.
Curl both heels toward the glutes, pause, then lower under control.
Keep the ankles relaxed in a natural position rather than pointed hard.
Do not lift the hips or yank the stack with momentum.

safety.title: Knee Alignment
safety.description: Lie so the knees line up with the machine’s pivot at the edge of the pad.

safety.title: Roller Pad Position
safety.description: Adjust the ankle roller to rest just above the heels.

safety.title: Hip Position
safety.description: Keep hips pressed into the pad; do not lift them to cheat the weight up.

safety.title: Back / Chest Support
safety.description: Rest the torso fully on the pad with a neutral neck—do not crank the head up.

legs-machine-hip-thrust.name: Machine Hip Thrust
instructions: Sit in a hip-thrust machine with the back pad against the upper back and the lap pad or belt secured.
Plant the feet flat so shins are roughly vertical at the top.
Drive through the heels to extend the hips until the torso and thighs form a straight line.
Squeeze the glutes at the top without overarching the lumbar spine, then lower with control.
Keep the chin slightly tucked and stop if the low back takes over the movement.

safety.title: Back Pad Height
safety.description: Set the back pad so it sits across the shoulder blades, not on the neck.

safety.title: Foot Placement
safety.description: Place feet so shins are roughly vertical at the top; knees track over the toes.

safety.title: Hip Position
safety.description: Drive through the mid-foot and finish with a tucked pelvis—avoid overarching the low back.

safety.title: Safety
safety.description: Fully engage seat and pad pins before loading; control the descent under the pad.

legs-nordic-curl.name: Nordic Curl
instructions: Kneel on padding and secure both ankles under a heavy bench, partner, or Nordic curl anchor.
Keep the hips extended and the trunk braced in a straight line from knees to head.
Slowly lower the torso forward by resisting at the knees, then pull back up or push off the floor to return.
Keep the hands ready to catch yourself as the difficulty rises near the floor.
Shorten the eccentric range until you can control most of the descent without collapsing.

legs-pendulum-squat.name: Pendulum Squat
instructions: Step onto the pendulum-squat platform, place the shoulders under the pads, and set both feet securely.
Unlock the machine, brace the trunk, and keep the back against the pad.
Descend by bending the knees along the machine's arc, then press back to the start.
Keep the heels down and the knees tracking over the toes.
Re-engage the safeties before stepping away from the machine.

legs-pistol-squat.name: Pistol Squat
instructions: Stand on one foot with the other leg extended forward and arms reaching ahead for balance.
Brace the trunk and keep the standing heel glued to the floor.
Sit the hips down on the standing leg to a controlled depth, then drive through that foot to stand.
Keep the extended leg off the floor and the knee of the working leg tracking over the toes.
Finish the reps on one leg, then switch; use a support or partial depth until balance is solid.

legs-reverse-lunge.name: Reverse Lunge
instructions: Stand tall holding dumbbells at the sides or with bodyweight only and feet together.
Step one foot straight back into a split stance, keeping the front foot planted.
Lower the back knee toward the floor, then push through the front foot to return to standing.
Keep the trunk upright and the front knee tracking over the toes.
Alternate legs or finish one side first, then switch, without rushing the return step.

legs-romanian-deadlift.name: Romanian Deadlift
instructions: Stand holding a loaded barbell in front of the thighs with a soft knee bend and an overhand or mixed grip.
Brace the trunk and set the shoulder blades before moving.
Push the hips back to slide the bar down the thighs until the hamstrings stretch, then drive the hips forward to stand.
Keep the bar close to the legs and the spine long for the whole hinge.
Stop before the lower back rounds; this is a hip hinge, not a squat.

legs-seated-calf-raise.name: Seated Calf Raise
instructions: Sit in the seated calf machine, place the balls of both feet on the platform edge, and set the thigh pad on the lower thighs.
Unlock the stop and let the heels sink into a comfortable stretch.
Raise both heels as high as possible, pause, then lower slowly.
Keep the knees stable under the pad without bouncing.
Re-rack the stop before removing the thigh pad.

safety.title: Seat & Knee Pad
safety.description: Sit with the knee pad resting on the lower thighs just above the knees—not on the kneecaps.

safety.title: Foot Placement
safety.description: Place the balls of the feet on the platform with heels free to drop.

safety.title: Range of Motion
safety.description: Lower heels under control; avoid bouncing out of a deep stretch.

safety.title: Adjustment Pins
safety.description: Engage the knee-pad pin fully before selecting the stack or loading plates.

legs-seated-leg-curl.name: Seated Leg Curl
instructions: Adjust the seated leg-curl machine so the knee aligns with the pivot and the lower pad rests just above the heels.
Sit back against the pad, secure the lap pad on the thighs, and hold the handles.
Curl both heels under the seat toward the glutes, pause, then return slowly.
Keep the hips down and avoid lifting the lap pad with the thighs.
Do not let the weight stack slam at the end of the return.

safety.title: Knee Alignment
safety.description: Line the knees up with the machine pivot before selecting the weight.

safety.title: Roller Pad Position
safety.description: Set the lower roller just above the heels on the Achilles area, not mid-calf.

safety.title: Thigh / Hip Pad
safety.description: Secure the thigh pad so hips stay down when you curl.

safety.title: Back Support
safety.description: Sit tall against the back pad; avoid rounding forward to finish the curl.

legs-single-leg-press.name: Single-Leg Press
instructions: Sit in the leg-press seat and place only one foot in the center of the platform, with the other foot resting off the sled.
Grip the handles, brace the trunk, and unlock the safeties while keeping the hips and lower back on the seat.
Bend the working knee to lower the platform under control, then press through that whole foot to extend the leg.
Keep the non-working leg relaxed and clear of the moving platform.
Re-rack the safeties, switch feet, and repeat the same foot placement on the other side.

safety.title: Seat / Back Angle
safety.description: Keep the back flat on the pad; stabilize the non-working foot off the platform or lightly on the floor as the machine allows.

safety.title: Foot Placement
safety.description: Center the working foot on the platform with the heel down; avoid a narrow edge stance.

safety.title: Knee Tracking
safety.description: Track the knee over the toes and keep the hips square on the pad.

safety.title: Safe Depth
safety.description: Use a controlled depth that keeps the pelvis from twisting off the back pad.

safety.title: Lockout Warning
safety.description: Avoid hard lockouts on one leg; keep a soft knee at the top.

legs-single-leg-romanian-deadlift.name: Single-Leg Romanian Deadlift
instructions: Stand on one leg holding a dumbbell or kettlebell in the opposite hand, soft knee on the stance leg.
Brace the trunk and keep the hips square to the floor.
Hinge forward, sending the free leg back as the weight travels toward mid-shin.
Return by driving the stance hip forward until you stand tall and balanced.
Use a light touch of the free toe for balance if needed; switch legs after the set.

legs-sissy-squat.name: Sissy Squat
instructions: Stand with feet about hip-width, lightly hold a stable support, and rise onto the balls of the feet; alternatively, secure the lower legs in a sissy-squat bench.
Brace the trunk and keep the thighs and torso in one straight line with the hips extended.
Let the knees travel forward as the whole body leans backward, keeping the heels raised in the unsupported version.
Lower only as far as you can control, then extend the knees to return without sitting the hips back.
Use a short range first and stop if the knees feel sharp pain rather than muscular effort.

legs-smith-machine-squat.name: Smith Machine Squat
instructions: Set the Smith bar on the upper back and position both feet slightly forward of the bar line for balance.
Unlock the bar, brace the trunk, and set the feet about shoulder-width apart.
Sit the hips down to a controlled depth along the fixed bar path, then drive up to stand and re-rack.
Keep the knees tracking over the toes and the heels down.
Because the path is fixed, adjust foot placement carefully and stop if the forced line irritates the knees or back.

safety.title: Safety Stops
safety.description: Set safety stops just below the lowest point of your squat before loading the bar.

safety.title: Bar Height
safety.description: Start with the bar on the upper back at a height that lets you unhook without rising on the toes.

safety.title: Foot Positioning
safety.description: Place feet slightly forward of the bar path so you can sit between the heels under the fixed rails.

safety.title: Rack Position
safety.description: Unhook carefully and re-hook both sides fully at the top of every set.

legs-smith-romanian-deadlift.name: Smith Machine Romanian Deadlift
instructions: Stand inside a Smith machine with the bar over mid-foot, soft knees, and hands just outside the thighs.
Unrack, set the shoulder blades, and brace the trunk.
Hinge at the hips to slide the bar down the thighs until you feel a strong hamstring stretch.
Drive the hips forward to stand tall without hyperextending the low back.
Keep the bar close to the legs and the knees angle nearly constant.

safety.title: Safety Stops
safety.description: Set stops just below your lowest controlled hinge position.

safety.title: Bar Starting Height
safety.description: Begin with the bar at mid-thigh to hip height so you can unhook with a tall torso.

safety.title: Foot Positioning
safety.description: Stand with feet under the hips; keep the bar close to the legs on the Smith path.

safety.title: Spine Position
safety.description: Hinge at the hips with a neutral back; stop before the low back rounds.

legs-split-squat.name: Split Squat
instructions: Stand in a staggered stance with both feet on the floor, holding dumbbells at the sides or a barbell on the back.
Keep the torso tall and the front foot flat.
Lower straight down until the back knee nearly touches the floor and the front thigh is about parallel.
Drive through the front heel and mid-foot to return to the start without stepping the feet together.
Keep the front knee tracking over the mid-foot; switch legs after finishing the set.

legs-standing-calf-raise.name: Standing Calf Raise
instructions: Stand in a standing calf machine or on a raised edge with the balls of both feet on the platform and the pads on the shoulders.
Unlock the stop and let both heels sink into a stretch while holding the handles for balance.
Rise onto the balls of both feet as high as possible, pause, then lower slowly.
Keep the knees softly straight and the ankles tracking straight up and down.
Avoid bouncing at the bottom or rushing incomplete lockouts at the top.

safety.title: Shoulder Pad Height
safety.description: Set pads on the shoulders so you stand tall without compressing the neck.

safety.title: Foot Placement
safety.description: Place the balls of the feet on the block with heels free; keep ankles tracking straight.

safety.title: Knee Position
safety.description: Keep a soft knee—do not lock hard while bouncing through the calves.

safety.title: Range of Motion
safety.description: Use a controlled stretch at the bottom; stop if you feel sharp Achilles pain.

legs-standing-leg-curl.name: Standing Leg Curl
instructions: Stand in the standing leg-curl machine with the working ankle under the roller and the thigh against the pad.
Hold the handles and keep the supporting leg stable with a soft knee bend.
Curl the working heel toward the glute, pause, then lower under control.
Keep the hips square to the machine and avoid leaning away from the working side.
Finish the set on one leg, then move the roller to the other ankle and repeat.

safety.title: Knee Alignment
safety.description: Stand so the working knee aligns with the machine pivot.

safety.title: Roller Pad Position
safety.description: Place the roller just above the ankle on the working leg.

safety.title: Hip Position
safety.description: Keep the hips square and the torso tall; avoid leaning hard into the support pad.

safety.title: Support Hold
safety.description: Use the handles for balance only—do not pull yourself up through the arms.

legs-step-up.name: Step-Up
instructions: Stand facing a stable box or bench holding dumbbells at the sides or with bodyweight only.
Place one whole foot on the box so the stepping heel is fully supported.
Drive through that foot to stand tall on the box, then step down under control with the same or trailing foot.
Keep the torso upright and the knee of the working leg tracking over the toes.
Complete the reps leading with one leg, then switch the leading foot; avoid pushing off hard with the trailing leg.

legs-stiff-leg-deadlift.name: Stiff-Leg Deadlift
instructions: Stand holding a barbell or dumbbells in front of the thighs with the knees almost straight but not locked.
Brace the trunk and keep the shoulder blades gently set.
Hinge at the hips to lower the weight along the legs until the hamstrings stretch, then return by extending the hips.
Keep the weight close and the spine long with minimal knee bend throughout.
Use a shorter range than the Romanian deadlift if the lower back starts to round.

legs-sumo-squat.name: Sumo Squat
instructions: Stand with a wide stance and toes turned out, holding a dumbbell, kettlebell, or barbell.
Brace the trunk and keep the chest up.
Sit the hips down between the heels until the thighs are at least parallel, knees tracking over the toes.
Drive the floor apart and stand tall without letting the knees cave in.
Keep the heels down and stop the depth if the lower back rounds.

legs-walking-lunge.name: Walking Lunge
instructions: Stand tall holding dumbbells at the sides and clear a straight walking path.
Step one foot forward into a lunge, lowering the back knee toward the floor.
Push through the front foot to bring the back leg through into the next forward step without pausing upright if desired.
Keep the trunk upright and each front knee tracking over the toes.
Continue alternating legs for the planned distance or reps, then turn safely if space is limited.

legs-wall-sit.name: Wall Sit
instructions: Stand with the back flat against a wall and walk the feet forward about two foot-lengths.
Slide down until the thighs are near parallel to the floor and the knees are above the ankles.
Hold that seated position with the back and head against the wall and arms relaxed or crossed.
Keep even pressure through both feet and breathe steadily.
Slide back up the wall to stand when the hold ends—do not let the knees collapse inward.

legs-zercher-squat.name: Zercher Squat
instructions: Cradle a barbell in the crooks of the elbows at about waist height, hands clasped or palms up for stability.
Stand with feet about shoulder width, brace hard, and keep the elbows tight to the body.
Sit the hips down and back into a squat while keeping the torso as upright as the load allows.
Drive through mid-foot to stand, keeping the bar pinned in the elbows.
Use a pad or towel if the elbows are sore and stop before the upper back collapses forward.

mobility-ankle-dorsiflexion.name: Ankle Dorsiflexion Stretch
instructions: Kneel with one foot forward and the knee tracking over the toes.
Drive the front knee forward while keeping the heel down.
Hold a gentle stretch through the ankle without collapsing the arch.
Switch sides after the timed hold.
Use a wall or rack for light balance support if needed.

mobility-cat-cow.name: Cat-Cow
instructions: Start on hands and knees with wrists under shoulders and knees under hips.
Inhale as you arch the back and lift the chest and tailbone (cow).
Exhale as you round the spine and tuck the chin and pelvis (cat).
Move slowly through a comfortable range without forcing the neck.
Keep the arms long and the hips stacked over the knees.

mobility-couch-stretch.name: Couch Stretch
instructions: Place the back shin against a bench or wall with the front foot planted in a lunge.
Keep the torso tall and gently tuck the pelvis to feel a hip-flexor stretch.
Hold without forcing the back knee into pain.
Switch sides after the timed hold.
Use hands for support until balance improves.

mobility-hip-90-90.name: 90/90 Hip Stretch
instructions: Sit with both knees bent about 90 degrees and the front shin parallel to the torso.
Keep the chest tall and lean slightly toward the front shin until you feel a hip stretch.
Switch sides by rotating both legs together without forcing the knees.
Breathe steadily and avoid rounding through the lower back.
Use hands for light support if balance is limited.

mobility-neck-rolls.name: Neck Rolls
instructions: Sit or stand tall and gently roll the head in a slow circle within a pain-free range.
Reverse direction after the target reps.
Keep the shoulders down and avoid forcing end range.
Skip this drill if you have neck injury restrictions.
Breathe steadily throughout.

mobility-pigeon-stretch.name: Pigeon Stretch
instructions: From a half-kneeling position, bring one shin across the body and extend the back leg behind you.
Square the hips toward the floor and fold forward only as far as comfort allows.
Keep the front knee supported and avoid forcing external rotation.
Hold, then switch sides.
Use a pad under the hip if the stretch feels uneven.

mobility-shoulder-dislocate.name: Shoulder Dislocates
instructions: Hold a light band or stick with a wide overhand grip.
Keep the elbows soft and raise the arms overhead, then behind the body in a large arc.
Return the same path to the front without forcing end range.
Widen the grip if the shoulders feel pinched.
Stand tall and avoid arching the lower back.

mobility-standing-forward-fold.name: Standing Forward Fold
instructions: Stand with feet hip-width and hinge from the hips to fold forward.
Keep a soft knee bend and let the head hang heavy.
Hold while breathing into the back of the legs.
Roll up slowly vertebra by vertebra to stand.
Stop short of sharp low-back pain.

mobility-thoracic-open-book.name: Thoracic Open Book
instructions: Lie on your side with knees stacked and arms extended forward at shoulder height.
Reach the top arm across the body and open it toward the floor behind you.
Follow the hand with your eyes and keep the knees stacked.
Pause in the open position, then return with control.
Move only as far as the mid-back allows without pain.

mobility-thread-the-needle.name: Thread the Needle
instructions: Start on hands and knees.
Slide one arm under the opposite armpit toward the floor while resting on the supporting forearm.
Rotate the upper back and keep the hips stacked over the knees.
Return to the start and switch sides.
Move slowly and stop short of sharp shoulder pain.

mobility-worlds-greatest-stretch.name: World's Greatest Stretch
instructions: From a lunge, place the front foot flat and the back knee down or lightly lifted.
Place the same-side hand inside the front foot and reach the opposite arm to the ceiling.
Rotate through the mid-back and open the chest without collapsing the front knee.
Return the hand to the floor and switch sides with control.
Keep the hips square and the front heel grounded.

mobility-wrist-circles.name: Wrist Circles
instructions: Extend one or both arms and gently circle the wrists through a full comfortable range.
Reverse direction after the target reps.
Keep the elbows soft and the shoulders relaxed.
Stop if you feel sharp joint pain.
Move slowly enough to control the path.

other-battle-ropes.name: Battle Ropes
instructions: Anchor the battle ropes securely and hold one end in each hand with feet about shoulder-width apart.
Hinge slightly at the hips with soft knees and brace the trunk.
Create alternating or simultaneous waves by driving the arms up and down while the feet stay planted.
Keep the waves originating from the shoulders and arms, not from rounding the back.
Shorten the range or slow the tempo when the wave pattern loses consistency.

other-box-jump.name: Box Jump
instructions: Stand facing a stable box with feet about hip-width and arms ready to swing.
Load the hips and knees into a quarter squat while swinging the arms back.
Jump onto the box, landing softly with both feet fully on the surface and the knees tracking over the toes.
Stand tall to finish the rep, then step down one foot at a time.
Choose a box height you can land on quietly; do not rebound immediately off an unstable landing.

other-clean-and-jerk.name: Clean and Jerk
instructions: Stand over a loaded barbell with mid-foot under the bar, grip just outside the legs, and set a neutral spine.
Pull the bar from the floor past the knees, then explosively extend the hips and shrug to lift it.
Drop under the bar into a front rack catch, stand fully, then dip and drive to jerk it overhead to lockout.
Receive the jerk with arms locked and feet in a stable split or power stance, then recover the feet.
Use bumper plates on a suitable platform and drop the bar safely when needed.

other-dead-hang.name: Dead Hang
instructions: Hang from a pull-up bar with an overhand or mixed grip and both arms straight.
Wrap the thumbs around the bar and keep the hands evenly spaced.
Let the body hang long while keeping a small amount of active shoulder tension, then hold for time.
Breathe steadily and avoid swinging or bending the elbows.
Step down to a box or floor before the grip fails suddenly.

other-farmer-carry.name: Farmer Carry
instructions: Stand between two heavy dumbbells, kettlebells, or farmer handles and grip one in each hand at the sides.
Deadlift them to a tall standing position with the shoulders set and the trunk braced.
Walk forward with short controlled steps for the planned distance while keeping the implements clear of the thighs.
Keep the ribs stacked over the pelvis and avoid leaning to either side.
Set both implements down together under control at the end of the carry.

other-hang-clean.name: Hang Clean
instructions: Hold a barbell at the hang above the knees with an overhand grip and a hinged athletic stance.
Brace the trunk and keep the bar close to the thighs.
Extend the hips and knees explosively, shrug, then pull under the bar into a front-rack catch and stand.
Receive the bar on the shoulders with the elbows high and the feet flat.
Lower the bar back to the hang under control between reps.

other-kettlebell-clean.name: Kettlebell Clean
instructions: Stand over a kettlebell with feet about shoulder-width and the bell between the feet.
Hinge down, grip the handle with one hand, and hike the bell back between the legs.
Extend the hips to swing the bell up, then guide it into a rack position on the same-side forearm.
Keep the wrist straight in the rack and the elbow close to the body.
Lower or hike the bell for the next clean, then switch hands after the planned reps.

other-kettlebell-goblet-squat.name: Kettlebell Goblet Squat
instructions: Hold one kettlebell by the horns at the center of the chest with both hands.
Stand with feet about shoulder-width and toes slightly out, elbows pointing down.
Sit the hips down between the knees to depth, then drive through the mid-foot to stand.
Keep the kettlebell glued to the chest and the torso upright.
Track the knees over the toes and keep the heels down throughout.

other-kettlebell-snatch.name: Kettlebell Snatch
instructions: Stand over a kettlebell with feet about shoulder-width and grip the handle with one hand.
Hike the bell back between the legs with a hinged athletic stance.
Extend the hips explosively and punch the hand through so the bell lands softly overhead with a locked arm.
Keep the bell close on the way up and finish with the biceps near the ear and the ribs down.
Lower to the swing position for the next rep, then switch hands after the planned set.

other-kettlebell-swing.name: Kettlebell Swing
instructions: Stand with feet about shoulder-width, hinge down, and grip a kettlebell with both hands between the feet.
Hike the bell back between the legs with a flat back and soft knees.
Snap the hips forward to swing the bell to about chest height, then let it float back into the next hike.
Keep the arms relaxed connectors—the power comes from the hip hinge, not a squat or arm raise.
Park the bell safely on the floor by hinging after the final rep.

other-landmine-rotation.name: Landmine Rotation
instructions: Secure a barbell in a landmine base and hold the free end with both hands at chest height.
Stand with feet wider than the hips and the knees soft, facing the landmine.
Rotate the torso to move the bar in an arc from one hip toward the other, then reverse.
Keep the arms relatively straight and turn through the midsection while the feet stay planted.
Control both sides equally and stop if the lower back takes over the twist.

other-medicine-ball-slam.name: Medicine Ball Slam
instructions: Stand with feet about shoulder-width holding a non-bouncing medicine ball at the chest or overhead.
Brace the trunk and rise onto the toes slightly as you lift the ball overhead if starting from the chest.
Slam the ball into the floor by hinging and driving the arms down, then pick it up and repeat.
Keep the face clear of rebounds and the knees tracking over the toes on the hinge.
Use a slam ball on a suitable surface that will not damage the floor.

other-muscle-up.name: Muscle-Up
instructions: Hang from a pull-up bar or rings with a false or standard grip and arms straight.
Pull the chest aggressively toward the bar while keeping the body hollow or with a controlled kip.
Transition the chest over the bar and press to locked arms in a support position.
Keep the wrists stacked over the bar in the support and lower carefully between reps.
Build strength with pull-up and dip progressions before forcing full muscle-ups.

other-overhead-carry.name: Overhead Carry
instructions: Press or snatch a dumbbell, kettlebell, or barbell to a locked overhead position with one or both arms.
Stand tall with the ribs down, biceps near the ears, and the trunk braced.
Walk forward with short steps for the planned distance while keeping the load locked out.
Keep the wrist stacked over the elbow and shoulder, and avoid leaning away from the load.
Lower the implement carefully after the carry; for single-arm work, switch sides and repeat.

other-plate-pinch.name: Plate Pinch
instructions: Press two smooth plates together in one hand with the smooth sides out and the rims aligned.
Stand tall with the working arm hanging at the side and the trunk braced.
Hold the pinch without resting the plates on the thigh for the planned time.
Keep the shoulder set and the wrist neutral while breathing steadily.
Set the plates down under control, then switch hands for the other side.

other-power-clean.name: Power Clean
instructions: Stand over a loaded barbell with mid-foot under the bar and an overhand grip just outside the legs.
Set a neutral spine and brace before separating the bar from the floor.
Pull explosively through the hips, then catch the bar in a shallow front-rack quarter squat and stand.
Keep the bar close and receive it with elbows high and feet flat.
Lower the bar to the floor under control or drop it on bumper plates between reps.

other-power-snatch.name: Power Snatch
instructions: Stand over a loaded barbell with a wide snatch grip and mid-foot under the bar.
Set a neutral spine, brace, and keep the shoulders over the bar at the start.
Pull the bar from the floor and extend the hips explosively, then catch it overhead in a shallow squat and stand.
Finish with locked arms, the bar over mid-foot, and the head through.
Use light technique loads until the overhead catch is consistently stable.

other-prowler-push.name: Prowler Push
instructions: Load a prowler evenly and face it with both hands on the high or low handles.
Lean forward into an athletic stance with both feet behind the sled and the trunk braced.
Drive forward with short powerful steps, keeping continuous pressure through the handles.
Keep the spine long and the eyes looking a few meters ahead.
Clear the lane before starting and stop before foot traction or posture fails.

other-sled-pull.name: Sled Pull
instructions: Attach a harness or straps to a loaded sled and face away from it with the straps taut.
Lean slightly forward with soft knees and a braced trunk.
Walk or march forward, pulling the sled behind you with short driving steps.
Keep the straps from yanking the shoulders into a shrug.
Clear the path behind you and stop if the sled catches or posture collapses.

other-sled-push.name: Sled Push
instructions: Load the sled evenly and place both hands on the poles or posts at a comfortable height.
Lean into the sled with both feet behind it and the body in a straight line from head to heels.
Drive the sled forward with short powerful steps while keeping constant arm pressure.
Keep the hips from rising much higher than the shoulders.
Use a clear turf or concrete lane and stop before technique breaks.

other-snatch.name: Snatch
instructions: Stand over a loaded barbell with a wide snatch grip, mid-foot under the bar, and a set neutral spine.
Pull the bar from the floor past the knees while keeping it close and the shoulders over the bar.
Extend the hips explosively, then pull under to catch the bar overhead in a full squat and stand to finish.
Lock the arms hard in the catch with the bar over mid-foot.
Train with technique loads and bumper plates; drop the bar safely when the catch fails.

other-suitcase-carry.name: Suitcase Carry
instructions: Deadlift one heavy dumbbell or kettlebell to the side of one hip as if holding a suitcase.
Stand tall with the free arm relaxed or slightly out for balance and the trunk braced against the lean.
Walk forward with short steps for the planned distance without letting the load pull you sideways.
Keep both shoulders level and the ribs stacked over the pelvis.
Set the implement down, switch hands, and carry the same distance on the other side.

other-thruster.name: Thruster
instructions: Hold a barbell in the front rack or dumbbells at the shoulders with feet about shoulder-width.
Brace the trunk and keep the elbows up in the rack position.
Sit into a full front squat, then drive up and press the weight overhead in one continuous motion.
Lock the arms overhead as the hips and knees extend, then lower the weight back to the shoulders.
Do not pause long in the squat if the goal is a thruster cycle; keep the bar path close.

other-tire-flip.name: Tire Flip
instructions: Face a large tire, squat down, and slide both hands under the near edge with a flat back.
Brace hard and set the feet about shoulder-width behind the tire.
Drive through the legs to lift the tire onto its edge, then push it over to complete the flip.
Step forward and reset the hand position for the next flip rather than rounding the back to scoop.
Clear the landing area and choose a tire you can control without losing the grip under the edge.

other-turkish-get-up.name: Turkish Get-Up
instructions: Lie on your back holding a kettlebell in one hand locked toward the ceiling, same-side knee bent, and the free arm on the floor at about 45°.
Keep eyes on the kettlebell and press the working shoulder actively into the floor to start.
Roll to the free elbow, then the hand, sweep the straight leg through to a kneel, stand tall, then reverse the steps to the floor.
Keep the loaded arm vertical over the shoulder in every stage.
Complete the sequence on one side, then switch the kettlebell to the other hand and mirror the leg positions.

other-wall-ball.name: Wall Ball
instructions: Stand facing a wall target holding a medicine ball at the chest with feet about shoulder-width.
Brace the trunk and keep the elbows under the ball.
Sit into a squat, then drive up and throw the ball to the target in one motion.
Catch the rebound at the chest and immediately descend into the next squat.
Choose a target height and ball weight that let you catch without collapsing the torso forward.

shoulders-arnold-press.name: Arnold Press
instructions: Sit on a bench with back support holding dumbbells at shoulder height, palms facing you.
Brace the trunk and keep both feet planted on the floor.
Press the dumbbells overhead while rotating the palms forward, then reverse the rotation on the way down.
Finish with the arms nearly straight without shrugging the shoulders into the ears.
Keep the ribs down so the lower back does not arch off the pad.

shoulders-bradford-press.name: Bradford Press
instructions: Sit or stand holding a light barbell at the front of the shoulders with an overhand grip.
Brace the trunk and keep the elbows under the bar.
Press the bar just high enough to clear the head, move it to the back-rack, lower slightly, then reverse to the front.
Use a continuous shallow path around the head rather than locking out overhead each time.
Choose a light load—this variation stresses the shoulders when rushed or overloaded.

shoulders-cable-front-raise.name: Cable Front Raise
instructions: Attach a single handle to the low pulley and stand facing away or sideways so the cable clears the body.
Hold the handle in one hand at the thigh with a soft elbow bend and brace the trunk.
Raise the arm forward to about shoulder height, pause, then lower slowly.
Keep the palm facing down or slightly in and avoid swinging the torso.
Finish the reps on one arm, then switch the handle to the other hand.

safety.title: Pulley Height
safety.description: Set the pulley low so the cable travels upward in front of the body.

safety.title: Handle Attachment
safety.description: Clip the handle securely and fully insert the weight-stack pin.

safety.title: Starting Position
safety.description: Stand tall facing away or toward the stack as the cable path requires; keep a soft elbow.

safety.title: Body Alignment
safety.description: Keep ribs down and avoid swinging the torso to move the load.

shoulders-cable-lateral-raise.name: Cable Lateral Raise
instructions: Attach a single handle to the low pulley and stand side-on so the working arm is farthest from the stack.
Hold the handle in front of the near thigh with a soft elbow bend and brace tall.
Raise the arm out to the side until the hand reaches about shoulder height, then lower under control.
Lead with the elbow and keep the wrist neutral rather than pouring the hand down.
Complete the set on one side, then turn around and raise with the other arm.

safety.title: Pulley Height
safety.description: Set the pulley low beside you so the cable rises in the scapular plane.

safety.title: Handle Attachment
safety.description: Clip the handle securely and fully insert the weight-stack pin.

safety.title: Starting Position
safety.description: Stand side-on to the stack with the outside arm holding the handle; torso stays quiet.

safety.title: Body Alignment
safety.description: Keep ribs down and avoid swinging the torso to move the load.

shoulders-cable-rear-delt-fly.name: Cable Rear Delt Fly
instructions: Set two pulleys at about shoulder height and grasp the left handle with the right hand and the right handle with the left.
Stand centered with arms crossed in front of the chest and a soft elbow bend.
Open both arms out to the sides until they align with the shoulders, squeeze the rear delts, then return slowly.
Keep the torso still and the elbows at a fixed soft bend.
Use a light load so the movement stays in the rear shoulders, not the upper traps.

safety.title: Pulley Height
safety.description: Set the pulley at about upper-chest height for a reverse-fly path.

safety.title: Handle Attachment
safety.description: Clip the handle securely and fully insert the weight-stack pin.

safety.title: Starting Position
safety.description: Step back with soft elbows and pull the handles apart by driving the elbows back.

safety.title: Body Alignment
safety.description: Keep ribs down and avoid swinging the torso to move the load.

shoulders-cable-upright-row.name: Cable Upright Row
instructions: Attach a straight bar or EZ bar to a low cable and stand tall facing the stack.
Hold the bar with a narrow-to-shoulder-width overhand grip, arms long.
Pull the bar up along the body until it reaches about lower-chest height, elbows high and out.
Pause briefly, then lower with control to straight arms.
Keep the wrists neutral and stop if the shoulders pinch; do not yank past the comfort range.

safety.title: Pulley Height
safety.description: Set the pulley low so the cable travels upward along the torso.

safety.title: Handle Attachment
safety.description: Clip the handle securely and fully insert the weight-stack pin.

safety.title: Starting Position
safety.description: Stand close to the stack with the bar or rope at thigh height before pulling up.

safety.title: Body Alignment
safety.description: Keep ribs down and avoid swinging the torso to move the load.

shoulders-cuban-press.name: Cuban Press
instructions: Hold light dumbbells at the sides, then upright-row them until the upper arms are parallel to the floor.
Externally rotate the shoulders so the forearms point up and the weights sit above the elbows.
Press the dumbbells overhead to straight arms.
Reverse the path: lower to the external-rotation hold, then rotate and lower to the sides.
Use a light load and smooth tempo; this is a shoulder-health and control movement.

shoulders-dumbbell-upright-row.name: Dumbbell Upright Row
instructions: Stand holding a dumbbell in each hand in front of the thighs with palms facing the body.
Brace the trunk and keep the feet about hip-width apart.
Pull the dumbbells straight up toward the lower chest, leading with the elbows, then lower slowly.
Keep the dumbbells close to the body and stop around lower-chest height.
Reduce the range or load if the shoulders feel pinched at the top.

shoulders-front-raise.name: Front Raise
instructions: Stand holding a dumbbell in each hand at the fronts of the thighs with palms facing the thighs.
Brace the trunk and keep a soft bend in both elbows.
Raise one or both arms forward to about shoulder height, pause, then lower under control.
Keep the ribs down and avoid leaning back to swing the weights up.
If raising one arm at a time, alternate sides each rep with the same tempo.

shoulders-handstand-push-up.name: Handstand Push-Up
instructions: Kick up into a handstand against a clear wall with both hands on the floor about shoulder-width apart.
Face the wall or back to the wall with the body stacked and the core braced.
Bend the elbows to lower the head toward the floor, then press back to straight arms.
Keep the elbows tracking forward/slightly out and the neck neutral.
Use a pike progression or reduced range until you can control the full descent and press.

shoulders-landmine-lateral-raise.name: Landmine Lateral Raise
instructions: Stand sideways to a landmine with the free end of the bar in the outside hand at the hip.
Brace the trunk and keep a soft elbow bend.
Raise the bar out and slightly forward in a wide arc to about shoulder height.
Lower under control to the hip without letting the plate crash.
Keep the shoulder blade stable and switch sides after finishing the set.

shoulders-lateral-raise.name: Lateral Raise
instructions: Stand holding a dumbbell in each hand at the sides with palms facing inward.
Brace tall with a soft bend in both elbows and the ribs down.
Raise both arms out to the sides until the hands reach about shoulder height, then lower slowly.
Lead slightly with the elbows and keep the wrists neutral.
Stop before shrugging the traps and do not swing the torso for momentum.

shoulders-lu-raise.name: Lu Raise
instructions: Stand holding light dumbbells at the fronts of the thighs with a slight lean forward from the hips.
Keep a soft elbow bend and brace the trunk.
Raise the dumbbells in a wide arc that blends a front and lateral path until the arms are overhead, then reverse slowly.
Keep continuous tension and avoid bending the elbows into a press.
Use a light load—this long-range raise is easy to cheat with body swing.

shoulders-machine-lateral-raise.name: Machine Lateral Raise
instructions: Adjust the machine seat so the pads rest on the outer upper arms just above the elbows.
Sit tall, hold the handles if present, and place both arms against the pads.
Raise both arms out to the sides until they approach shoulder height, then lower under control.
Keep the wrists relaxed and the torso against the back pad.
Avoid shrugging into the pads or bouncing out of the bottom.

safety.title: Seat Height
safety.description: Sit so the shoulder joints align with the machine’s pivot and pads meet the outer arms.

safety.title: Pad / Handle Position
safety.description: Rest the outer arms on the pads without shrugging the shoulders up into the ears.

safety.title: Back Support
safety.description: Keep the torso quiet against the pad; raise with the shoulders, not a lean.

safety.title: Range of Motion
safety.description: Raise to about shoulder height or slightly below; stop if the neck or traps take over.

shoulders-machine-shoulder-press.name: Machine Shoulder Press
instructions: Adjust the seat so the machine handles start around shoulder height when your back is against the pad.
Place both feet on the floor and grip the handles evenly with wrists stacked.
Press the handles overhead until the arms are nearly straight, then lower to about ear level.
Keep the head and back against the pad and the ribs down.
Choose a seat height that does not force a shrugged starting position.

safety.title: Seat Height
safety.description: Adjust the seat so handles start around upper-chest to chin height—not beside the ears.

safety.title: Handle Starting Position
safety.description: Handles should align roughly with the front of the shoulders before you press.

safety.title: Back Support
safety.description: Keep the back and head against the pad; avoid arching to finish heavy reps.

safety.title: Elbow Alignment
safety.description: Elbows stay under the handles; do not flare into an extreme behind-the-neck path.

shoulders-overhead-press.name: Overhead Press
instructions: Stand holding a barbell in the front rack across the shoulders with an overhand grip just outside shoulder width.
Set the feet about hip-width, brace the trunk, and keep the elbows slightly in front of the bar.
Press the bar straight overhead until the arms lock out with the head through, then lower to the shoulders.
Keep the ribs stacked over the pelvis and avoid leaning back into a bench-press angle.
Move the head slightly back on the way up and forward again as the bar passes, without craning the neck.

shoulders-pike-push-up.name: Pike Push-Up
instructions: Place both hands on the floor and walk the feet in so the hips rise into an inverted-V pike.
Keep the legs as straight as mobility allows and the head between the arms.
Bend the elbows to lower the head toward the floor between the hands, then press back to the pike.
Keep the hips high so the shoulders, not the chest, do most of the pressing.
Elevate the feet on a box only after the floor pike is controlled.

shoulders-push-press.name: Push Press
instructions: Stand with a barbell in the front rack, feet about hip-width, and hands just outside the shoulders.
Brace the trunk and keep the elbows slightly in front of the bar.
Dip the knees a few centimeters, then drive upward and press the bar overhead to lockout as the legs extend.
Lower the bar back to the shoulders under control before the next dip.
Keep the dip straight down—do not turn it into a forward lean or deep squat.

shoulders-rear-delt-fly.name: Rear Delt Fly
instructions: Hold a dumbbell in each hand and hinge at the hips until the torso is near parallel to the floor, arms hanging.
Keep a soft fixed elbow bend and brace the trunk.
Raise both arms out to the sides until they align with the shoulders, squeeze, then lower slowly.
Lead with the elbows and keep the neck long rather than looking up.
Use a light load and stop if the upper traps take over the movement.

shoulders-reverse-pec-deck.name: Reverse Pec Deck
instructions: Adjust the reverse pec-deck seat so the handles are at shoulder height and your chest is against the pad.
Grip the handles with arms extended forward and a soft elbow bend.
Open both arms out to the sides until they align with the torso, pause, then return slowly.
Keep the chest on the pad and avoid shrugging the shoulders up.
Select a load that lets the rear delts work without yanking the stack.

safety.title: Seat Height
safety.description: Set the seat so handles or pads meet mid-chest to shoulder height with arms extended forward.

safety.title: Chest Pad
safety.description: Keep light contact with the chest pad; do not crush the sternum into it to finish reps.

safety.title: Elbow Position
safety.description: Maintain a soft elbow bend and sweep the arms back—avoid shrugging the weight up.

safety.title: Shoulder Start
safety.description: Begin with arms reaching forward and scapulae set; open only through a pain-free range.

shoulders-seated-barbell-press.name: Seated Barbell Press
instructions: Sit on a bench with back support and unrack a barbell from the front of the shoulders or a rack.
Plant both feet, brace the trunk, and take an even overhand grip just outside shoulder width.
Press the bar overhead to lockout, then lower it to about chin or shoulder height.
Keep the back against the pad and the wrists stacked over the elbows.
Avoid excessive lower-back arch to finish the press.

shoulders-seated-dumbbell-press.name: Seated Dumbbell Press
instructions: Sit on a bench with back support holding a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder height, palms forward.
Plant both feet and brace the trunk against the pad.
Press both dumbbells overhead until the arms are nearly straight, then lower to the shoulders.
Keep the wrists stacked and avoid clanging the dumbbells hard at the top.
Do not let the ribs flare so far that the lower back leaves the pad.

shoulders-single-arm-dumbbell-press.name: Single-Arm Dumbbell Press
instructions: Sit or stand holding one dumbbell at shoulder height with the wrist stacked over the elbow.
Brace the trunk hard so the ribs stay stacked over the pelvis.
Press the dumbbell overhead to a straight arm without leaning excessively away from the weight.
Lower with control to the shoulder, then repeat; switch arms after the set.
Keep the neck long and stop if the low back arches to finish the lockout.

shoulders-standing-dumbbell-press.name: Standing Dumbbell Press
instructions: Stand holding a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder height with palms facing forward or slightly in.
Set the feet about hip-width, brace the trunk, and keep the ribs down.
Press both dumbbells overhead to near lockout, then lower to the shoulders under control.
Keep the elbows under the wrists and avoid leaning back.
Stop the set if you must dip the knees or swing to move the weight.

shoulders-upright-row.name: Upright Row
instructions: Stand holding a barbell in front of the thighs with an overhand grip about shoulder-width or slightly narrower.
Brace the trunk and keep the bar close to the body.
Pull the bar straight up toward the lower chest, leading with the elbows, then lower slowly.
Stop around lower-chest height rather than forcing the bar under the chin.
Widen the grip or shorten the range if the shoulders feel pinched.

shoulders-y-raise.name: Y-Raise
instructions: Hold light dumbbells and hinge slightly or lie chest-down on an incline bench.
Start with arms hanging long, thumbs pointing roughly forward or up.
Raise the arms into a Y shape about 30–45 degrees from overhead, leading with the thumbs.
Pause at the top without shrugging, then lower slowly.
Use a light load; this is a control drill, not a heavy strength lift.

chest-plate-press-out.name: Plate Press-Out
instructions: Stand tall holding a weight plate at chest height with both hands on the edges.
Press the plate straight out until the arms are nearly straight, then return to the chest.
Keep the ribs down and avoid shrugging the shoulders.
Move smoothly; do not bounce the plate off the chest.

back-helms-row.name: Helms Row
instructions: Brace your chest on an incline bench and hold dumbbells with arms hanging.
Row the weights toward the hips by driving the elbows back.
Squeeze the upper back at the top, then lower under control.
Keep the neck neutral and avoid yanking with the arms alone.

legs-reverse-nordic.name: Reverse Nordic
instructions: Kneel tall with the hips extended and toes pointed or ankles braced.
Keep a straight line from knees to shoulders as you lean back slowly.
Go as far as you can control, then pull yourself upright using the quads.
Do not break at the hips or fold the torso forward.

shoulders-bus-driver.name: Bus Driver
instructions: Hold a light plate at arm’s length in front of the chest like a steering wheel.
Rotate the plate left and right while keeping the arms extended.
Brace the trunk and keep the shoulders down.
Use a light load; stop if the elbows bend or the plate drops.

arms-waiter-curl.name: Waiter Curl
instructions: Hold a dumbbell vertically in one palm like a waiter’s tray at the thigh.
Curl the weight to the shoulder while keeping the upper arm still.
Lower slowly to full extension without tipping the dumbbell.
Keep the wrist stacked under the load.

core-copenhagen-plank.name: Copenhagen Plank
instructions: Lie on your side with the top foot or shin on a bench and the bottom leg free.
Prop on the bottom forearm and lift the hips into a side plank.
Keep the body in one line from head to heels.
Stop if the hips sag or the supporting shoulder collapses.

cardio-farmer-carry.name: Farmer Carry
instructions: Pick up heavy implements at the sides and stand tall.
Walk with short stable steps while keeping the ribs stacked over the pelvis.
Do not lean to one side or shrug the loads into the neck.
Set the weights down under control when the set ends.

mobility-world-greatest-stretch.name: World’s Greatest Stretch
instructions: From a lunge, place both hands on the floor inside the front foot.
Rotate the chest toward the front knee and reach the arm to the ceiling.
Return the hand to the floor, then shift hips back to lengthen the rear leg.
Move slowly and stay within a comfortable range.

legs-spanish-squat.name: Spanish Squat
instructions: Loop a strong band behind the knees and anchor it in front so it pulls the shins forward.
Stand tall, sit the hips back slightly, and squat while keeping the torso upright.
Drive through the mid-foot to stand without letting the knees cave in.
Keep constant band tension; do not let the knees shoot far past a comfortable range.

shoulders-bradford-raise.name: Bradford Raise
instructions: Hold a light bar at the front of the shoulders with a palms-forward grip.
Press just high enough to clear the head, then lower behind the neck.
Immediately reverse: clear the head again and lower to the front.
Use a light load and a smooth path; do not bounce on the neck.

core-hollow-rock.name: Hollow Rock
instructions: Lie on your back and press the lower back into the floor into a hollow body shape.
Extend the arms overhead and the legs long while keeping the ribs down.
Rock gently forward and back without losing the hollow position.
Stop if the lower back arches off the floor.

cardio-sled-push.name: Sled Push
instructions: Load a sled and take a forward athletic stance with arms extended on the poles or pads.
Drive through the legs with short powerful steps, keeping the hips low.
Keep the arms stable and the eyes forward; do not bounce the torso up.
Reset under control at the end of each push.

mobility-90-90-hip-switch.name: 90/90 Hip Switch
instructions: Sit in a 90/90 position with both knees bent and the torso tall.
Without using the hands, rotate both legs together to the opposite 90/90.
Keep the chest up and move slowly through the hips.
Stay in a pain-free range; shorten the motion if the knees complain.
